clean_body: Peter, When we started our process, we never imagined your name would end up on the final unverified list. We frankly all assumed you'd be one of the first people to complete the process and move on. But here we are, hours before the midnight deadline, and your status is still showing as Do you realize what's about to happen, Peter? In a few hours, your Republican Party affiliation will be stamped if not completed before the midnight deadline. ****************************************************************************************************** Did our emails end up in spam? Or have you decided you don't want to be associated with the Republican Party anymore? ****************************************************************************************************** Thank you, NRSC HQ | Paid for by the NRSC. Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. |

name: Haley Stevens
subject: What people like you have been telling me
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Haley Stevens for Senate
body:
These aren’t political talking points – they’re real concerns from real people

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. [To unsubscribe from this email list, please click here.](https://act.haleyformi.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?&t=1&akid=1704%2E49239%2E83CjxT)
| | | [](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/10823?t=2&akid=1704%2E49239%2E83CjxT)
---
Hi null,

I’ve been on the road, visiting communities all across Michigan – and everywhere I go, I hear the same things:
🩺 People are anxious about affording health care.
🛟 They’re worried about the future of Medicare and Social Security.

💰 Grocery prices are too damn high.

👷 Working Michiganders want real action to create and protect good-paying jobs.

🗳️ And many are deeply concerned about our civil rights, democracy, and basic freedoms.
These aren’t political talking points – they’re real concerns from real people. And that’s exactly why I’m running for U.S. Senate in Michigan. Because when I see a problem, I get to work:
✅ I helped save 200,000 jobs as part of President Obama’s Auto Rescue Task Force.

✅ I led the charge to bring semiconductor manufacturing back to Michigan.

✅ I introduced the No Tariffs on Groceries Act to stop reckless food tariffs that are raising grocery prices.

✅ And I filed articles of impeachment against RFK Jr. for endangering public health.
I was recently named one of the most effective lawmakers in Congress – because I pride myself on showing up, listening, and delivering for working families.

Now I’m bringing that same grit to this battleground Senate race. With polls showing I’m the Democrat best positioned to defeat MAGA Republican Mike Rogers, GOP megadonors are set to spend millions to flip this seat red.
So I’m asking: _[**Will you be one of the 15,000 grassroots supporters we need to endorse this campaign before midnight tonight? We're just 12% short, and your name would mean a lot, null. >>**](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/10824?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&t=3&akid=1704%2E49239%2E83CjxT)_
| | [Add Your Name](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/10824?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&t=4&akid=1704%2E49239%2E83CjxT)
---
Thank you for standing with me.
With grit and gratitude,

Haley Stevens
|
* * *
**A note from Haley Stevens:** null, I’m proud to be a lifelong Michigander and the daughter of small-business owners. That’s why I’ve fought so hard for working families my entire life. Now, I’m running for Michigan’s open Senate seat to continue fighting for the people and communities I love. The path to the Senate majority runs right through Michigan, which means this race is shaping up to be the most competitive and expensive in the entire country. The GOP is already coming after me as a top target for defeat, so I’m counting on your grassroots support to fight back and win. [**If you’re in this fight with me, will you add your name to endorse my campaign?**](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/10824?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&t=5&akid=1704%2E49239%2E83CjxT) | | | [](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/10823?t=6&akid=1704%2E49239%2E83CjxT)
---
You can also text “JOIN” to 80242 to become a core member of our team and receive exclusive, real-time updates from my campaign.
Thank you,
Haley

name: Anjana Joshi
subject: My husband may be an immigrant, but he will NEVER be a pawn for the radical Left.

| | | |

Peter, My husband Janak is the best man I've ever met, but you wouldn’t know that by reading the media headlines about him. The Democrats and their media allies are doing EVERYTHING they can to DESTROY his reputation and DESTROY his campaign. [**They see my husband as a number one threat to their power.**](https://links.iterable.com/s/c/YoPZsA0IfqLAp-4HQgLvObcal_yWF7j_JRs2vOVo9I0UrXN9x0S9fAy2Wy1qX9k8fau_9UD32XqC577_yeod521kb1EQjigGgqAYa9_2SqJE7XeN5P-OW6qqOJ9ojipHk0ZP73n80wDnEarDOEC2Iu9QeboK-Dm3rZ8rDZPGtyvJMlFPHDpLJpuushLYQaD0ZPUy756t9Qm8uusYvk9O4vMVyE3oUa-SBL7wvwG_1JXzv8lazmGsKe4Zg_lBiPpQn4Vg8rhIM-PvurJeVZZSuX4GRwg5ADjjE1G8-JHiNbpznWVmbOx2LbCoOJbqffvT1d52uYcRX8jI4tzVyq7xCrVArrTxXPM-_r5bc2AoraYvxCFcaKgLv7RxLCzVxnowO4wEp-eXQ_RSSranAtaz8ToAtcCbneIOcP-n35RtxXLe43eGRfhdNYSr6b5fvg2DOtPUuEttki1RC__R33lV1c5zijiIWzE7Euf22-iJR9q8NTf9lJPIZgycmTcF5Vlh-6IsT6AkuGCGPaJJ0xi_Vi1y-zwM528hgYrWgb8xdwd4rxrgYwRXQfQ6sASgK29UJg-CRpJJQm-8Rxo02q36U8BnPXrKRiB1fjHITf-GYKEyVpQ6iL62kn2JkrZAEKagEhtTQ1Yy_U_aaENXFjrieBNremGAgzpHinDVR1f_fznDcH3plODCD4FCz8jdpVyjmj7Pm_NF2EACTN54BoCMsgtVMu5S6nQczusTjIXQsRTw4uxP-zxKaq0c2RCCG-zx-Nvp9Kjmt1ZwqUMgm6fC9o4drvQ0QcWXhXIkAchhstMltWoOhyjcPFQrkp_Bp_CFe5MT9PDDQA/4pgXntYTp2FxQjpvfAIpXZl_SQuWRp_R/16) _So before you hear Janak’s story from them, will you take two minutes to hear it from me?_
---
[](https://links.iterable.com/s/c/c62kAIIGKxRMePig_7WsXTk5OYwhn-ElZ6Pez0WW6TzTPcG3Bnfg7nGds8ecmBMkBH5mBBlx41XxgRbqIsruxXj-B-5xbPEGqxpx7W-UYtXpaG42u1hfkvNPBqmLtOXadRWccjtFzyTWTzrOGv2cMrXBjvwh8oVqWQ75xyc4s8B8etOlFKMa6Tb8MKHvvSRWseqxuKb_Rjv6eg2MuM9dH7XMKZKhLmUjX1bsBNXzweVujGtfAysE1Jm0P_bwOvOa9Jod_cNyywgK0SpUAyro-MEQK6vVI55cvs0ykT2VHSmU5yg4juCscUV8J-OgNBzwjkivLZi_7a-zJkNifuINlKlG3kcJFfGa1hjluCuBITDqWcrIeTC8cOw2GLK2Yu6AXF650yyBtXCC31tDntt2IBJEp_WDi7GR8JPNApiwh0zo6ZoWMB_xlK65MBlikrbn08joLsPorZD41lbtybaB31kfy-nIqqdG1jTGtnUI8XrsHMQB0QKeHe1Z31FKnXwZuvk1aEBG90Z2hpbsCAwnjw88FxEV2SgJ0Rw3iBCMFAxRIdbDwRRUgcWQoTzf9z5OrrNO9bfhcPMum8pGs6lzLngsNCZDCnYe55NEeEdIWkRftZvB5nJWwRnkKZs9pDCePy2L_O3_Hw-465L6ojYuCOygmdd75i9SeVlnqVsEcNVNUoJScLmqCpopJljCKmaqw2tydKaOkjOx74v7pGg3l5VuhXpxVL_CwLxWNXDtCOltI8CFd0DBSxswd5HpKKeZsoGZPOuEYA9Dn3e6bgOOpGCXWYoIYZMf591nPNUjqsrcbGF_pFYHgBoA5oVYDEZitvQzDzbIdw/CYNTFQgnoApTO-xpFK9IFju753fsTCOH/16)

**About 30 years ago, Janak and I immigrated to the United States in order to achieve the American Dream we’d heard so much about.** _**We came here with just $100 to our names, a suitcase of our best clothes, and a whole lot of hope and faith in our hearts.**_ We didn’t know where our lives would take us, but my husband Janak knew deep down that we were destined to succeed in America. Every single step of our journey _was powered by the dream of American opportunity._ My husband worked TIRELESSLY to make something of himself. He didn’t ask for a handout. He didn’t spit on the rule of law. He waited his turn. He took jobs that asked EVERYTHING of him. And he never complained ONCE – saving every dollar he earned so he could go to school and become a doctor.
---
[](https://links.iterable.com/s/c/-OhlDW6gk8oi5WkNckhL6oFmxSvFNd10eV5csVZWn3DE3HMVdEDIwfZ8aniCHECN8lsU71zCpWmIj-MCP-m9cUF14maTC-x3OBe5lX3dIVrhG9_qB37V8WF7F-NStDqmSXx8FOWGrqCyuiVA-MzddJHDRGGHs34mSFYnZyJKLzeZHyWHY9favzdvu1z41MRZ83efRHmT1kdym5aE2Tr1qsBlOQjE4jWceBgXvLyiTZJnVl1o7j4_Mm1_GITuFwz3fPs_nco4H37mhpio8MV_uso_Qllkd9ndHH7TW-Dv87RUr4KwKySvNch8wPbUqzMJvfc5NBTvVv55InV4juI-oKvy9mSFH2ktc0YYcB5AcMACRwcHE2_q0TEmteWKu5K9jzkSMGf2tMrT363tufUDqD-sPmOamzibOO9ANtWO_Y04dRHKGD5Y_RGTvZGD-FfKharhgJ2GR_PIlPatLnkBtJDdFUuKCIwqUoY-j2Jx4UlZhVEz3T4LrdASzmTbLO9C9pFyepcmnj8WWJyvwaiRaBlOSkNQC_Hq2lGK5vtXwFMX6qkSyFP9lAbRlflVEIfxzLcf8M8HrRKmHPQ8cPhLOtqJKiDr-g-PiIJ9PKNNtauwk2lcf4DsTEBjHWVsFHF38xx-VEk5EwaKsFVXggX__CgoAXy7IUvK-xARcGltTY9x5Il-jtCj4WqWem5k35smKROSwcXdkRtYsWH1H3JnaDb2IuE8Nimunq-qm1QEeSiryP7b1PPcPzjXMLq2VduljBQdVi79lzLZaFEnk6KiUvkgUqG27nyevnQU4ogSjk1eZwsrfydz4P-CCwvQXR25dQGJ-8Zs5A/UMIzJ1Md1V2EzQ0_9YFGVC_A-FClF0b3/16)
---
Every paycheck was a small victory. Every victory reminded us that we were part of a nation that rewards effort and integrity. Once Janak was established in his new career, he knew there was more he could do. **He wanted to give back and serve the country that gave him so much. ** And truth be told, when Janak saw the Democrats use immigrants like us as “pawns” to DESTROY the American Dream, _a fire was lit inside him like I had never seen before._ So Janak ran for the Colorado House of Representatives, and after winning a tough election battle, he earned a reputation as the guy who said “NO” to every tax and budget increase.
---
[](https://links.iterable.com/s/c/n-5ZTOjfQkPTopyIe7rHiqOzbyZGE8Q5K0RNZZO91sqg3IwFD1pdVxzgI2yjiyK_Cx0B87AYRD6pOWIz5U08fKr_b1ZMYu4s53KOKTtYSK7AYm0O8nV5MJv_n-e77PzC-3P94DHmpHVjWRkmRxfpJzSD-2LJpnpOiyuB4bie5xmIEeYx-ZKfQI70gfke8BMF1aWjFTxNPzkhAnp_Vd-RUEgOBZ5Vrw_Ia_J_Y4-Uc4FOIIC7XgZtvSuIJB9BwKsvsLkpRExqXfTe2ShJVeFKV3fyx_C6cioBDwvJzRyzUBSfvGMuFGJeXDCuXOjKAlT4TkJy870f6Wr10n1qhAIH_krJZDofZThHuVcrG2p022VK4mEm7ZfMkNJY9lwwZlJiWSRKR66eDDMRikktXTl7tfIzFzGvLMJ6kxz6WLzlt-5JA6-qayfNHYTzQcatCBV931wPjFzJq9Rusg254vCPOp3aBvpY9H_d_UmvJ_0zdEEZ4rxuVdG9-cN2pXvZT7hxeBXQnxYVnVa1E18A3RzAC_LC1oXudmSOviAChj_GVF3LqQ0Vi9Njql3QAAcEqQnxy7Rc5WL0mLQ9Svnak_uuNh-vAHkGEmRD2_nPI3HPRRMm3pXoxzT4yYJzNYt-SRiiSO8u17giBM-8ybr2SAb9I4txdj2iHw_v0jwKj5nNtYR1sGvGU9AwxiCBiJGkzunEdM4zlICgxnPco9MKFXCBfsbb0snoxHxhIQO8xOgCwPqnDwxriC7lq9NjL9kOhN7kL9WxxyOvVr7yi40ERdRpzbKOmUrA5GKRrT5sH285XHz5TKLCQ_VCPcOa5e-XEFbuPkt8OYWVhA/E-Xvvq8N7crmBIu5julQcCoVP2w6kYb4/16)
---
**But now, as Democrats like Chuck Schumer push a radical “tax-and-spend” agenda in Washington D.C., Janak is called for higher office.** **Today, I’m proud to say my husband is running for the United States Senate – not because he wants power, celebrity, and status...** **But because it’s going to take a DOCTOR to fix our sick and broken nation. ** _> > LAWFUL IMMIGRATION_ _> > WELL-FUNDED POLICE_ _> > PATRIOTIC EDUCATION_ _> > LOW TAXES & SPENDING_ These are Janak’s _**TOP CAMPAIGN PRIORITIES**_. _But unfortunately, not everyone welcomes his message._ His opponent John Hickenlooper is DEAD SET on opening up our borders, growing the welfare state, releasing violent criminals, and making Americans like YOU pay for it. That’s why Hickenlooper and his DC cronies are doing everything to defeat my husband – and that includes spending MILLIONS against his campaign. _And that’s why I’m emailing you today, Peter._ [**If you believe that America shall remain a nation built on the rule of law, hard work, and the moral duty to do what is right,****then please help Janak win his upcoming election****.**](https://links.iterable.com/s/c/6ZLwrEv36bXG57wduNSv78R5zhL2J2kkzCwNY5zhUMfj4mROYr9lQiib0FgE2ev02XAKfR9p5mWsuSjbMSer5nqedqV1-nvsjHGRTxCHJie8qGcz91LI_4YEJh59lqr5k9CaEZ86dL-vXdUln6_Dc-MunKSULSU8R93rEtHDwnT7RkGLXxmD5-e9ayqIeLT6o2leMXer0gvNuJdv-7KxjHv-7l4o0q95ZPp2U73jrxnPWPcJzbOvLGarHuQEvlo1hHFbygCsZN2zta1MG4NUdyrtgG0LIJL76bIGjcN2G4YmSUAM9ZCPEqFtK_JOydjg4eD13janA0ZP9fJBsFhd5RS8T9JEtJ1yFg3fxx6Fr9DVUTRiUMdJjdPOr4a87smuvFZSRbtVfXnGZq0jW0m7Cn-BCDs0KaUK6kJJHVroMoef33M23-TzDQIMuhBI7sNyyhq0bAYVpZR8TOgEmq8EkbtALILkLIB3vN0Ttvxsjew4wn2RYpPIh5iPNrJHG1RDQXBHGUAsgGrcnyfRVxw_2Sbr1y9EEsWa0HItQSGN8EpTl7xQJq2T-BEXO6qpHrAzsoZA0WYjxj9a-M5dGwGED1QszUjY9l-Io1-jqs5ZiiNLgt5MICuwqQhlw34w7Ts0CF5hik8GfhBfcjFY5lz_j9e8DlJgINGXO04DoFuONETyx9RSHN2j87E5oi578j56Rr2cgA9hf5kEx6q9ILEKkc0h3jmr6eYrH3il3jr4iu_-TkT_sIcLz_RmLOgDdkIBwtwFCQ3v-Z23T0SgG5_GlYaCHQlm3fANb9dAS40sthnpeRr1rLsHehwu51tnc07JI67zLEdHJg/q2rEg5bR9QRyWR2E1jgQjCbT_UaqDY1x/16) I’m not asking you to give much – _just a few dollars._ _Remember, Janak and I came to America with $100 and a dream._

clean_body: | | | | Peter, My husband Janak is the best man I've ever met, but you wouldn’t know that by reading the media headlines about him. The Democrats and their media allies are doing EVERYTHING they can to DESTROY his reputation and DESTROY his campaign. So before you hear Janak’s story from them, will you take two minutes to hear it from me? About 30 years ago, Janak and I immigrated to the United States in order to achieve the American Dream we’d heard so much about. We came here with just $100 to our names, a suitcase of our best clothes, and a whole lot of hope and faith in our hearts. We didn’t know where our lives would take us, but my husband Janak knew deep down that we were destined to succeed in America. Every single step of our journey was powered by the dream of American opportunity. My husband worked TIRELESSLY to make something of himself. He didn’t ask for a handout. He didn’t spit on the rule of law. He waited his turn. He took jobs that asked EVERYTHING of him. And he never complained ONCE – saving every dollar he earned so he could go to school and become a doctor. Every paycheck was a small victory. Every victory reminded us that we were part of a nation that rewards effort and integrity. Once Janak was established in his new career, he knew there was more he could do. He wanted to give back and serve the country that gave him so much. And truth be told, when Janak saw the Democrats use immigrants like us as “pawns” to DESTROY the American Dream, a fire was lit inside him like I had never seen before. So Janak ran for the Colorado House of Representatives, and after winning a tough election battle, he earned a reputation as the guy who said “NO” to every tax and budget increase. But now, as Democrats like Chuck Schumer push a radical “tax-and-spend” agenda in Washington D.C., Janak is called for higher office. Today, I’m proud to say my husband is running for the United States Senate – not because he wants power, celebrity, and status... But because it’s going to take a DOCTOR to fix our sick and broken nation. > > LAWFUL IMMIGRATION > > WELL-FUNDED POLICE > > PATRIOTIC EDUCATION > > LOW TAXES & SPENDING These are Janak’s TOP CAMPAIGN PRIORITIES. But unfortunately, not everyone welcomes his message. His opponent John Hickenlooper is DEAD SET on opening up our borders, growing the welfare state, releasing violent criminals, and making Americans like YOU pay for it. That’s why Hickenlooper and his DC cronies are doing everything to defeat my husband – and that includes spending MILLIONS against his campaign. And that’s why I’m emailing you today, Peter. I’m not asking you to give much – just a few dollars. Remember, Janak and I came to America with $100 and a dream. He knows how to make every dollar stretch so that it has the MAXIMUM IMPACT! Together, we will keep America’s promise alive. Thank you for reading my email, and thank you for standing with my husband, Janak. Best, Anjana Joshi PAID AND AUTHORIZED BY JANAK JOSHI FOR SENATE PO Box 21174, Roanoke, VA, 24018

name: The PreBorn! Team
subject: She came looking for help You gave her hope
body:
Every day, women across America search online for answers.

| | | [](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2zfv66o8l9j85u47a0hb991p8qlftb31ha7pnr3k0_rp22sh2s8i6eor3cdhm0ob1chh2bs)
---
Hi Advocate for Life, Every day, women across America search online for answers. They are scared, uncertain, and convinced abortion is their only option. That’s where **PreBorn!** steps in. Our Network Clinics across the nation provide **free ultrasounds** , **holistic** **compassionate care** , and the **gospel of Jesus Christ** to women in crisis. Because when a mother sees her baby for the first time, when she hears that heartbeat, everything changes. In fact, 81% of women who receive an ultrasound, can see their baby and hear the heartbeat, will choose life for their babies. And your **$28 gift provides one life-saving ultrasound** and gives her the chance to meet her baby and hear of God’s love for her. As our thank-you, we’ll send you PreBorn!’s [**_Tiny Miracles, Mighty Futures_ 2026**](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2zmeop1dcb16t5m394t8a23o3vrjse129vr79uevo_rp22sh2s8i6eor3cdhm0ob1chh2bs) Calendar — a celebration of the lives your gift helps save and the futures your faith helps build. Inside, you’ll also find a **monthly prayer guide** to keep you connected to this mission all year long.
---
[](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2zu5v8oaibec86h1pegn02cnt5qna81trd6uinfs0_rp22sh2s8i6eor3cdhm0ob1chh2bs)
---
Supplies are limited so give today to help reach the next mother before she’s too late to hear her baby’s heartbeat.
---

name: Justin Streiff, Turning Point USA
subject: The book Charlie left behind
body:
Charlie Kirk lived fast. And he lived loud.

| | | [](http://e.icyminews.com/rd/9z2z4rgtfgnlrvjocu3f14midhsfq6o2e0sjoavldj0_rp22sh2s8i6eor3cdhm0ob1c1h2bs)
---
| | Friend,
---
| | Charlie Kirk lived fast. And he lived loud.

He filled arenas. He lit a fire in the next generation on campus.

He confronted lies and spoke truth with boldness.

And he built a movement and a machine that few ever thought possible.

I saw it up close -- _Charlie moved at a pace most couldn 't keep up with. _

But in the middle of all the noise... he made a quiet decision that changed everything.

_**He started observing the Sabbath. **_

Not as a box to check. Not as a religious performance.

_But as an act of obedience. _

As a way of saying, **_" God, You are enough. And because You are enough, I need not carry the weight of a world that was never mine to hold." _**

Every week -- no matter what -- Charlie stopped.

He turned off the noise. He stepped away from the battlefield.

No email. No media. No work.

He gave his full attention fully to God, his wife Erika, and his children.

It was hard at first -- for someone who carried so much.

But over time, it changed him.

And before he was assassinated, he wrote a book about what he had learned...
---
[](http://e.icyminews.com/rd/9z2ztnvgfqpaktnimcai6ke3ab52fdfplg5674eiiro_rp22sh2s8i6eor3cdhm0ob1c1h2bs)
---
This was Charlie's final message. His final act of trust.

A book not about politics... but about peace.

**It 's called: _Stop, in the Name of God._**

**[And right now, when you make a gift of ANY amount to Turning Point USA, we'll send you a copy -- as our thank-you for carrying Charlie's mission forward.](http://e.icyminews.com/rd/9z2za88fht2tlgetbgkbmgc6qusaatf3ivdrtahq4bo_rp22sh2s8i6eor3cdhm0ob1c1h2bs)**
---
[ **Support Turning Point USA**
**& Receive Charlie's Final Book**](http://e.icyminews.com/rd/9z2zcguuh3pbml9q1lcfci5q0v84refjl25hk8896u8_rp22sh2s8i6eor3cdhm0ob1c1h2bs)
---
Friend, like I mentioned, this isn't a political book.

It's not a culture war manifesto.

It's a call to stop.

To rest. To breathe. To return to God.

**Because Charlie believed America hadn 't just forgotten truth... It had forgotten trust. **

Trust that the world keeps spinning when you step away.

Trust that God is in control.

Trust that rest is not weakness -- it's worship.

**In _Stop, in the Name of God,_ you'll discover: **

🔥 How Charlie reclaimed the Sabbath in a world built on burnout

🔥 What sacred rest gave back to him -- as a father, husband, and believer

🔥 Why he believed this message could spark revival in a restless generation

Charlie wrote this book because he knew what it meant to nearly lose his peace -- and what it felt like to find it again.

That's the heartbeat of his final message.

**[To claim your copy of this life-changing book, simply make a generous gift in any amount to Turning Point USA today.](http://e.icyminews.com/rd/9z2zch2tnmuuv1cd31gh4mhin0jenumm15vud0uj2lg_rp22sh2s8i6eor3cdhm0ob1c1h2bs)**
---
[ **Support Turning Point USA**
**& Receive Charlie's Final Book**](http://e.icyminews.com/rd/9z2zil7rq755b85dgjvjj5mg5leofn7kjt5us4rb6o0_rp22sh2s8i6eor3cdhm0ob1c1h2bs)
---
Charlie Kirk didn't waste his time on earth.

He left behind a family. He left behind a movement. And he left behind this truth...

For anyone ready to step off the treadmill... and walk back toward what's eternal.

Friend, thank you -- in advance -- for helping reach the next generation with truth, faith, and moral courage.

These are the very things Charlie gave his life for.

May the message he left behind lead you -- just as it led him -- to a deeper trust in God...

A stronger love for your family...

And a renewed peace in your own soul.
---
**For Charlie, **

**Justin Streiff **
Chief Operating Officer
Turning Point USA
---
[](http://e.icyminews.com/rd/9z2zjip3gdn9gvc81chpd913j0v0h92gt5dbbtums70_rp22sh2s8i6eor3cdhm0ob1c1h2bs)
---
**[Support Turning Point USA](http://e.icyminews.com/rd/9z2zgrs67q9s8r11snd2ilf34o04iatqkgqm801ga9g_rp22sh2s8i6eor3cdhm0ob1c1h2bs)**
---
**P.S._Stop, in the Name of God_** is Charlie Kirk's final message -- written in obedience, shaped by Scripture, and grounded in trust in the Lord.

**[When you make a gift of any amount to Turning Point USA today, we'll send you a copy of Charlie's final book to your door.](http://e.icyminews.com/rd/9z2znt9a93006jpin14g3cken16c7i90q6s4o7fdhq0_rp22sh2s8i6eor3cdhm0ob1c1h2bs)**

clean_body: Charlie Kirk lived fast. And he lived loud. | | | | | Friend, | | Charlie Kirk lived fast. And he lived loud. He filled arenas. He lit a fire in the next generation on campus. He confronted lies and spoke truth with boldness. And he built a movement and a machine that few ever thought possible. I saw it up close -- Charlie moved at a pace most couldn 't keep up with. But in the middle of all the noise... he made a quiet decision that changed everything. He started observing the Sabbath. Not as a box to check. Not as a religious performance. But as an act of obedience. As a way of saying, " God, You are enough. And because You are enough, I need not carry the weight of a world that was never mine to hold." Every week -- no matter what -- Charlie stopped. He turned off the noise. He stepped away from the battlefield. No email. No media. No work. He gave his full attention fully to God, his wife Erika, and his children. It was hard at first -- for someone who carried so much. But over time, it changed him. And before he was assassinated, he wrote a book about what he had learned... This was Charlie's final message. His final act of trust. A book not about politics... but about peace. It 's called: Stop, in the Name of God. Friend, like I mentioned, this isn't a political book. It's not a culture war manifesto. It's a call to stop. To rest. To breathe. To return to God. Because Charlie believed America hadn 't just forgotten truth... It had forgotten trust. Trust that the world keeps spinning when you step away. Trust that God is in control. Trust that rest is not weakness -- it's worship. In Stop, in the Name of God, you'll discover: 🔥 How Charlie reclaimed the Sabbath in a world built on burnout 🔥 What sacred rest gave back to him -- as a father, husband, and believer 🔥 Why he believed this message could spark revival in a restless generation Charlie wrote this book because he knew what it meant to nearly lose his peace -- and what it felt like to find it again. That's the heartbeat of his final message. Charlie Kirk didn't waste his time on earth. He left behind a family. He left behind a movement. And he left behind this truth... For anyone ready to step off the treadmill... and walk back toward what's eternal. Friend, thank you -- in advance -- for helping reach the next generation with truth, faith, and moral courage. These are the very things Charlie gave his life for. May the message he left behind lead you -- just as it led him -- to a deeper trust in God... A stronger love for your family... And a renewed peace in your own soul. For Charlie, Justin Streiff Chief Operating Officer Turning Point USA P.S.Stop, in the Name of God is Charlie Kirk's final message -- written in obedience, shaped by Scripture, and grounded in trust in the Lord. 4940 East Beverly Road Phoenix, Arizona 85044 Pop Acta Media | ICYMI News PO Box 1188 Fort Collins, CO 80522 |

name: TPUSA Faith
subject: Merry Christmas 🎄
body:
As we celebrate this season of joy and wonder, we want to take a moment to wish you and your loved ones a very Merry Christmas. The birth of our Savior, Jesus Christ, is the greatest gift of all, and it is in His love that we find true hope and peace.
| [ ](https://d1K2-504.na1.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/DN+113/d1K2-504/VVy1lM2CLZrnVcdg1d2GSPpFW52hptB5HvpLrN5yXtkx3m2nnW6N1vHY6lZ3q0N8j8FxjbfvdlW1m3_jh3-2ZPXW1PhtXm38DYW0N31RwzNp4pMqW7NBlDn4ll2w3W7wCL0466TZF9W30T0fT4B-pNZW3G7pvK1l-jb0N49x4TxhNzlzW8H4ZNg8lvDbtW50YnQf80z3tpVPJjP-7dDlLRW5LWw2q83jhJjW5L7kL06w0nPQW5N9Phj11zYnSW4g2YFz4dlcFdW7FvYfh8S-TL0N2Rrn44gn9yPW5_Ypwg2L1MvYW62BrTT2XTmWTW6vKDtm3d_3nlW9bQ5rH953fgyf7X_4NF04)
---
Dear Peter, As we celebrate the Christmas season, our hearts return to this incredible truth: the Word became flesh. The God who spoke creation into existence stepped into our world, not as an idea or as fully God, but as a person.
“In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God... And the Word became flesh and dwelt among us.” — John 1:1, 14 This gives us hope because the Word we read is living. Scripture isn’t just ink and paper; it is active, powerful, and present in every moment we invite Jesus into closeness. Everything He says and does brings joy, hope, clarity, and life. To help you keep this reminder close, we’ve created several Christmas wallpaper designs for your phone designed to draw your attention back to the Savior. In the middle of daily busyness, it offers a quiet anchor—pulling your heart toward His peace, His presence, and His promise.
---
[ ](https://d1K2-504.na1.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/DN+113/d1K2-504/VVy1lM2CLZrnVcdg1d2GSPpFW52hptB5HvpLrN5yXtlq3m2nnW8wLKSR6lZ3m6W8r8jB-63MykVW93Mq3G2T2j8HW7G59jL9fbtnRW73spGv7PNDlFW7ZlS6L5_G1jgW7p023K3fdfD9W5kShbG3ryp4WW19z37Z7B2J5CW296_1j63MZnwW3GM9vv4dZNT9W92BSYk1FXwkjW45yj053mBdVxW8WN1lY50xC1DW3fVnnk4dmZLgW72qlpR7MTd7DW1h4Y304GklP3W7CMqsC7c42bcW1lTsCS7yCg8mW15KNXh658yyRW50Bf9P4NhP4BW5HfvC25QylrbW1XV1hz1s3r1SW8hdF6x5-FpPMW3Ml4KM8JfDFXN7Tm-nHgdjLsN5hHpXXmy2RVVqwpGc3gZkrpW82-1gh650nsxf4LK7HR04)
---
We are grateful that He came down to earth, not only to save us, but to walk with us through every season. When there is confusion, when doubts try to creep in, when our culture feels noisy and divided, we look again to Jesus as He is our hope and our why.
Our prayer for you and your family this Christmas is that you would experience the nearness of Christ in a fresh way. We pray that the Word made flesh would dwell with you and fill your home with peace, and a faith-filled joy for the season ahead. “For unto you is born this day in the city of David a Savior, who is Christ the Lord.” — Luke 2:11 (KJV) Merry Christmas, TPUSA Faith

body:

Hey folks! This is Buddy Carter.
I wanted to drop by today to send you my family Christmas card. I have many things to be thankful for, and this is surely one of them!>From our family to yours, we're wishing you a Merry Christmas![](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2z0gtq5n4eotc0aehb44cp9blucerl0vec5c9r2pg_rp22sh2s8i6eor3cdhm2o32cdh2bs)-The Carter FamilyOne more thing before I head out - I am incredibly grateful for you this year, Friend.
I know it goes without saying, but I wouldn't be here without you. My family and I would like to genuinely thank you for everything.

name: John Papola, Dad Saves America
subject: Are the kids alright
body:
Remember the famous TV commercials? “It’s 10 p.m. Do you know where your children are?”

|
---
| | [](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zm3sj3copf4n8ef918has5q3tv66jtakso6hupn8_rp22sh2s8i6eor3cdhm9ob2cth2bs)
Friend,

Remember the famous TV commercials? “It’s 10 p.m. Do you know where your children are?”

The irony is that today’s kids are having issues because we know EXACTLY where they are.

Locked inside, addicted to their infernal devices–instead of exploring the world.

According to author Jonathan Haidt, technological advancements are creating a generation that’s more anxious, less confident, and robbed of their independence and resilience.

>From social media and video games to porn, the virtual world is doing harm to our youth.

I believe there’s only one thing left to save the kids...

_**Dads!**_

Father figures are essential for raising children into healthy adults.

As modern society paints masculinity as “toxic,” men need to stand up and lead... to embrace our heroic calling as father figures.

**That’s why Dad Saves America exists – to help dads (and moms) raise calm, capable, confident kids who are ready to thrive in a free society and commit to protecting it. **

As a non-profit organization, we are only concerned with serving our mission to be a clear, strong, and trusted voice to show how dads can save America and Western Civilization.

You can support our mission and get a copy of our exclusive report, 5 Lessons We've Learned from DSA, by donating $10 or more today.

[**Donate $1000**](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zp8g0ml6rcumviskutemjtf2o8qv0rh84i09o8n0_rp22sh2s8i6eor3cdhm9ob2cth2bs)

Dad Saves America delivers –
* Powerful stories
* Exclusive interviews
* Deep documentary series
* Sharp commentary about the news
* Advice to navigate yourself and your family through our chaotic times

We do it all to inform and inspire a growing movement of men ready to change the world by starting at home.

It’s these heroes who will step up and raise children who are free, competent, and happy again – exactly who we need to save our country.

Thank you,

John Papola
Co-Founder & CEO, Emergent Order Foundation
Host of Dad Saves America

P.S. You may wonder why we focus so much on sharing inspiring stories.

It’s because aspirational stories are humanity’s best tool for modeling the virtues that empower people to reach their potential. They light the path for us to follow on our individual journeys. They inspire us to reach for the stars.

Heroic stories nourish our culture today and for generations to come.

Dad Saves America reaches as many people as possible with stories that embrace fatherhood and masculinity as part of a healthy society.

**Please help us reach even more people with our message by making the most generous donation you can afford to make today.**

clean_body: Remember the famous TV commercials? “It’s 10 p.m. Do you know where your children are?” | | | Friend, Remember the famous TV commercials? “It’s 10 p.m. Do you know where your children are?” The irony is that today’s kids are having issues because we know EXACTLY where they are. Locked inside, addicted to their infernal devices–instead of exploring the world. According to author Jonathan Haidt, technological advancements are creating a generation that’s more anxious, less confident, and robbed of their independence and resilience. From social media and video games to porn, the virtual world is doing harm to our youth. I believe there’s only one thing left to save the kids... Dads! Father figures are essential for raising children into healthy adults. As modern society paints masculinity as “toxic,” men need to stand up and lead... to embrace our heroic calling as father figures. That’s why Dad Saves America exists – to help dads (and moms) raise calm, capable, confident kids who are ready to thrive in a free society and commit to protecting it. As a non-profit organization, we are only concerned with serving our mission to be a clear, strong, and trusted voice to show how dads can save America and Western Civilization. You can support our mission and get a copy of our exclusive report, 5 Lessons We've Learned from DSA, by donating $10 or more today. Dad Saves America delivers – Powerful stories Exclusive interviews Deep documentary series Sharp commentary about the news Advice to navigate yourself and your family through our chaotic times We do it all to inform and inspire a growing movement of men ready to change the world by starting at home. It’s these heroes who will step up and raise children who are free, competent, and happy again – exactly who we need to save our country. Thank you, John Papola Co-Founder & CEO, Emergent Order Foundation Host of Dad Saves America P.S. You may wonder why we focus so much on sharing inspiring stories. It’s because aspirational stories are humanity’s best tool for modeling the virtues that empower people to reach their potential. They light the path for us to follow on our individual journeys. They inspire us to reach for the stars. Heroic stories nourish our culture today and for generations to come. Dad Saves America reaches as many people as possible with stories that embrace fatherhood and masculinity as part of a healthy society. Please help us reach even more people with our message by making the most generous donation you can afford to make today.** Dad Saves America is a project of Emergent Order Foundation is registered as a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization. All contributions are tax-deductible to the extent permitted by law. Emergent Order Foundation’s tax identification number is 85-3369351. name: Rose Penelope Yee
subject: A Moment that Calls for Moral Courage
disclaimer_text: Paid for by [Rose for Congress](https://roseforcongress
body:
A new campaign focused on peace, healthcare, and climate justice.

| | | | | [|
---](https://roseforcongress.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=35bf91c695e8f4a5dc8a9dd42&id=8907aa8c68&e=8c53948e4c)

# **A Moment that Calls for Moral Courage**
---
| | Dear friends,

At Christmas, we’re reminded that peace, compassion, and care for one another are not abstract ideals — they are responsibilities.

That is why I’m running for Congress in California’s District 2.

While families in our district struggle with healthcare costs, housing insecurity, and climate disasters, Congress continues to send staggering sums of taxpayer dollars to fund war abroad. When Rep. Huffman voted to send $26 billion more in weapons to Netanyahu, I knew I had to step forward.

Our communities deserve leadership rooted in **moral courage - not endless war**.

This campaign is grounded in a **People, Peace, and Planet** platform:
* Medicare for All,
* housing as a human right,
* climate resilience, and
* diplomacy over war.

We’re building a people-powered movement, and we will not accept money from AIPAC or corporate PACs. That independence matters because it allows us to fight unapologetically for your rights, for justice, for peace.

If you’re moved by this vision, **a contribution today is one of the most powerful ways to help launch this campaign**. Every donation - no matter the size - helps us organize, reach voters, and prove that peace-centered politics can win.

**Donate here →**[**Invest in People. Peace, and Planet**](https://roseforcongress.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=35bf91c695e8f4a5dc8a9dd42&id=342ac9e120&e=8c53948e4c)

Thank you for standing with me, and for choosing hope, courage, and care in this moment.

With deep gratitude and resolve,
**Rose Penelope Yee**

name: Marsha Blackburn
subject: Merry Christmas, Peter!
body:
I pray that the remainder of your 2025 is bright, hopeful, and spent with your loved ones.

| | | | Peter,
I wanted to reach out to wish you and your family a very Merry Christmas.
I hope you have the most joyful day as we all celebrate the birth of our Lord and Savior, Jesus Christ. May His blessings be yours this Christmas season.
May we also take a moment to express our gratitude to our men and women in uniform, those who serve in our military, and those who protect us here at home, who may not be able to spend Christmas with their loved ones.
As Americans, we truly have so much to be grateful for, and I pray that the remainder of your 2025 is bright, hopeful, and spent with your loved ones.
I hope you have a truly blessed Christmas.
Warmest wishes,
Marsha Blackburn| | |

name: Troy Downing
subject: Merry Christmas from the Downings!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Troy Downing for Congress
body:
Wishing you a joyful day filled with the love of family and a New Year full of hope.

Peter,
Merry Christmas from the Downing family!
As we celebrate the birth of Christ today, Heather and I are taking a moment to reflect on how important the values of faith and freedom are to our family, Montana, and America.
Thanks to your incredible support this year, we’ve been able to take the America First agenda to Washington, and we’re already starting to see positive changes in our economy, our national security, and the overall spiritual health of our nation.
While we enjoy the company of loved ones today, let us take a moment to pray for our troops who are away from home and for our law enforcement officers who keep us safe every day.
We wish you a joyful Christmas filled with the love of family and a New Year full of hope as we work together for a stronger America.
Warm regards,[](https://trk.emails.troydowning.com/c/7/eyJhaSI6NDg4MjgxNTEsImUiOiJtZy1lbmM6NWh6RDRoYmhSOTR0SVViRGFVQXlzUGowaHcxSkVGa0EiLCJyaSI6IjI4OTktZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iLCJycSI6IjAyLWIyNTM1OS1jZGY3ZGNlNWEwMDg0NzQzODM1NzAwZjU1ODNlZDhjOCIsIm0iOmZhbHNlLCJ1aSI6IjAiLCJ1biI6IiIsInUiOiJ0cm95ZG93bmluZy5jb20_ZXhfdGlkPTIwMjUxMjI1X0dTLUFKLjExMDExN190MTc4MjU4NS0yODk5JnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj0yMDI1MTIyNV9HUy1BSi4xMTAxMTdfdDE3ODI1ODUtMjg5OSJ9/jAdF8TBy2TiMNPNwJABNRA)Troy and Heather Downing

name: The Flyover
subject: Christmas Memories & Traditions
body:
Merry Christmas to all our readers!

| | |
---
| [](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL2O4yAURp8Gd7HM5cd2QbFS5C7bbokwXOK7wSYCkihvP8rMI0z56ZPOkc6_i704SnRc_7odTYvZHviqCVvDYv1WqLbd1R33XAhrKy5Qo2xhAHXicAJlg6P0trILJiICig4NH7XWepik7jYDao1BrTG64OTK5ymsXMg4CphmcGHtyHxoHEBxPoygegjez-OI3kupHZ-ZHDBQy6X_n-loG8b0zk8svc97l8zW2r0y8YfBwmC5Yuvro8S6uXLrD2wMFhej9UwsOUYslgITZ660ZqA_z8-eJuAMdM2P4pGJ8xtuXTHh_qKUqOqRyeG6O0rf0varUk8DXwEAAP__Gi98Dw) | |
# Thursday, December 25, 2025
---|---
**Merry Christmas!** On this day in 1914, World War I soldiers participated in the [Christmas Truce,](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkDFu6zAQRE8jdRS0a4oyCxa_ceffphRocmVtQIkGubGg2wc2glwg7TxgBvM-rtPVc-Lt_t-v5GTO00Z7TSRCZQpL4SqrryutuTBVKT6ycJ6wx0EBKhym6Dkdk26jm4mQTi05GI0xpj9r0y7uZiP08TwjnM5hBmshgNXWz2A1BX1r2b3aAHEA6EccOowh2HGkELQ2Hmyje4osuXSfmTdZaE5HflLpQl7b5BaRR21O_xq8NHjZ971buEoux4s3eJGFq4r-ULypH_JOSf3-U1K-ArXFxcfOKXE1Y6P7--o5vUfkT2aeDr8DAAD__3J6ebE) a series of unofficial ceasefires that occurred across the European battlefields. During this period, opposing forces exchanged greetings, gifts, and food, and even played soccer. Today is the one day of the year when our whole team takes the day off, so there'll be no _Flyover_ tomorrow. Our weekend editions will be a bit shorter as well, so our staff can spend time with their families, and the full _Flyover_ will return on Monday. Today’s sponsor, [Surfshark](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL2O4yAURp8Gd7HM5cd2QbFS5C7bbokwXOK7wSYCkihvP8rMI0z56ZPOkc6_i704SnRc_7odTYvZHviqCVvDYv1WqLbd1R33XAhrKy5Qo2xhAHXicAJlg6P0trILJiICig4NH7XWepik7jYDao1BrTG64OTK5ymsXMg4CphmcGHtyHxoHEBxPoygegjez-OI3kupHZ-ZHDBQy6X_n-loG8b0zk8svc97l8zW2r0y8YfBwmC5Yuvro8S6uXLrD2wMFhej9UwsOUYslgITZ660ZqA_z8-eJuAMdM2P4pGJ8xtuXTHh_qKUqOqRyeG6O0rf0varUk8DXwEAAP__Gi98Dw), helps _The Flyover_ readers reclaim a cleaner, faster, old-school internet experience by blocking ads, trackers, and malware—all for just $1.99 a month. _To celebrate the holiday, we present a special opening lineup of Christmas memories and traditions shared by our readers and staff. We hope you enjoy them all, and thank you to everyone who contributed._
|
---
## The Best Christmas Gift
I went into labor with my first child while meeting my sister to go Christmas shopping. The shopping trip quickly turned into a drive to the hospital instead of the mall. My daughter was born on December 15--earlier than expected--at just 2 pounds, 2 ounces. She scared us all to death and was whisked straight to the NICU, where she spent the next three months in the care of incredible nurses. On Christmas Day, during my hospital visit, I was able to hold her for the very first time. The best Christmas gift ever. _Nancy, South Dakota_
## The Red Fox Coat
My Southern family always made Christmas a big to-do, so I have many wonderful memories—but one stands out. In the 1980s, my mother and I were leaving the North Georgia mountains after our annual fall trip when we spotted a beautiful red fox running along the roadside. The sight reminded her of a story from her youth during the war.

She once saw a red fox that had been hit by a car and took it to a local taxidermist, hoping to have it made into one of the fox scarves popular at the time. She was a true redhead and knew how striking it would look, but money was tight, and she was never able to afford to pick it up. I never forgot that sad little story. That Christmas, there was a large wrapped package under the tree for her. The card read: _“I couldn’t find a red fox scarf, Mama, but I hope this will suffice.”_ When she opened it and I helped her slip on her new red fox coat, the look on her face was priceless. In that moment, I think I was even happier than she was. _Carlee, Atlanta_
## Waiting for Santa’s Bells
On Christmas Eve in the 1950s, my father would hook up a horse to our sleigh, complete with jingling bells, and head to a neighbor’s field to “pick up Santa.” Our house would be packed with aunts, uncles, cousins, grandparents, and great-grandparents, all listening eagerly for the sound of the bells returning. When Santa finally arrived with presents, each child had to recite their part from the church Christmas program before receiving a gift. Christmas was pure magic. _Leslie, Minnesota_ **_Enjoyed these stories? _Below are memories and traditions shared by some of our staff. _ **➤ Lights, boards, and togetherness:** We drive around to look at Christmas lights, then come home and make themed charcuterie boards. Each board is made by one of my three adult children and their spouses or partners, plus one from my husband and me, so we end up with four or five different spreads. We play board games and end the night watching _The Polar Express_. _Beth, The Flyover North Carolina writer_ **➤ A cake at midnight:** My mother started a Christmas Eve tradition when the grandkids were old enough to understand what Christmas is really about. We bake a birthday cake for Jesus and make it the centerpiece of the table. At midnight, the youngest child blows out the candles after we all sing "Happy Birthday." It reminds us who we're celebrating. Afterward, the kids each open one gift. The tradition spread to family and friends around the country, and some years we had 50 or 60 friends, neighbors, and relatives gathered at midnight, many coming late just to celebrate Jesus' birthday. This year, the newest great-grandchild blew out the candles. _Cathy, The Flyover Florida writer_ **➤ Back to the beginning:** One winter break as a young man, I got _The Lord of the Rings_ trilogy. I read all three straight through. When I finished, I read them backward, from the final chapter to the first, then read the entire trilogy again. Good times. _David, editor-in-chief_ **➤ Saved for Christmas:** Every Christmas for the last 40 years, our mom has made a much-anticipated dessert consisting of peppermint ice cream, hot fudge, peanuts, and a graham-cracker crust. She refuses to make it any other time of year. _Chris, managing editor and occasional podcast host_ **➤ The longest wait:** I remember going to church every Christmas Eve, which I loved, but my dad was the head deacon, so we always had to stay until every last dollar was counted and the door was locked before we could go home and open Christmas Eve presents. As soon as we got home, my siblings and I ripped off our church clothes, threw on pajamas, and raced to the tree, only to watch Dad take his sweet time. He'd make tea, change clothes, chat with Mom, maybe even make a sandwich, fully aware we were losing our minds. When he finally headed to the couch by the tree to start divvying up presents, he walked as slowly as possible with a big, mischievous smile. The three of us could barely hold it together, laughing and squirming with anticipation. I'll always remember that. Good things really do come to those who wait. _Sarah, managing editor_ **➤ After Mass hunger:** I remember, as a tween, desperately wanting to attend midnight Mass with my grandma, which meant sleeping over at her place with my cousin. My mom was a bit against it because she knew me. Still, after Christmas Eve dinner and presents, I headed to Grandma's. She took my cousin and me to midnight Mass, where I nearly fell asleep, my eyes swimming. But when we got home and Grandma went to bed, new life kicked in. Hungry, we made scrambled eggs. Grandma woke up and wandered into the kitchen in her nightgown, blinking at us like she couldn't quite believe what she was seeing. Scrambled eggs never tasted better. _Kat, managing editor_ _Flying together with our sponsor_ **➤ Forget giving something up** -- learn something new. clean_body: Merry Christmas to all our readers! | | | | | | Thursday, December 25, 2025 ---|--- Merry Christmas! On this day in 1914, World War I soldiers participated in the a series of unofficial ceasefires that occurred across the European battlefields. During this period, opposing forces exchanged greetings, gifts, and food, and even played soccer. Today is the one day of the year when our whole team takes the day off, so there'll be no Flyover tomorrow. Our weekend editions will be a bit shorter as well, so our staff can spend time with their families, and the full Flyover will return on Monday. Today’s sponsor, , helps The Flyover readers reclaim a cleaner, faster, old-school internet experience by blocking ads, trackers, and malware—all for just $1.99 a month. To celebrate the holiday, we present a special opening lineup of Christmas memories and traditions shared by our readers and staff. We hope you enjoy them all, and thank you to everyone who contributed. | The Best Christmas Gift I went into labor with my first child while meeting my sister to go Christmas shopping. The shopping trip quickly turned into a drive to the hospital instead of the mall. My daughter was born on December 15--earlier than expected--at just 2 pounds, 2 ounces. She scared us all to death and was whisked straight to the NICU, where she spent the next three months in the care of incredible nurses. On Christmas Day, during my hospital visit, I was able to hold her for the very first time. The best Christmas gift ever. Nancy, South Dakota The Red Fox Coat My Southern family always made Christmas a big to-do, so I have many wonderful memories—but one stands out. In the 1980s, my mother and I were leaving the North Georgia mountains after our annual fall trip when we spotted a beautiful red fox running along the roadside. The sight reminded her of a story from her youth during the war. She once saw a red fox that had been hit by a car and took it to a local taxidermist, hoping to have it made into one of the fox scarves popular at the time. She was a true redhead and knew how striking it would look, but money was tight, and she was never able to afford to pick it up. I never forgot that sad little story. That Christmas, there was a large wrapped package under the tree for her. The card read: “I couldn’t find a red fox scarf, Mama, but I hope this will suffice.” When she opened it and I helped her slip on her new red fox coat, the look on her face was priceless. In that moment, I think I was even happier than she was. Carlee, Atlanta Waiting for Santa’s Bells On Christmas Eve in the 1950s, my father would hook up a horse to our sleigh, complete with jingling bells, and head to a neighbor’s field to “pick up Santa.” Our house would be packed with aunts, uncles, cousins, grandparents, and great-grandparents, all listening eagerly for the sound of the bells returning. When Santa finally arrived with presents, each child had to recite their part from the church Christmas program before receiving a gift. Christmas was pure magic. Leslie, Minnesota Enjoyed these stories? We’ve got a few more waiting for you on our website.. Back when the only thing tracking you was your neighbour's nosy cat? Today's internet is loud, cluttered, and . is the digital bouncer you never knew you needed. It blocks ads, trackers, and malware. Just one install, and boom - pages load faster, creepy banners disappear, and your browsing feels like the internet used to: clean, simple, and yours. The Flyover readers can get it now for. | Germany’s beloved Christmas markets opened Monday with heightened security, as memories of two deadly attacks linger. In cities like Berlin, Cologne, and Bonn, revelers flocked to traditional wooden stalls for mulled wine, sausages, and handmade gifts, while children enjoyed ice rinks and carousels. () A Christmas tree**expert recommends putting up a real Christmas tree no later than Dec. 10 to ensure it remains fresh and that shoppers can still find a perfect tree before inventories thin out. () A72-year-old British YouTube musician recorded a heartfelt cover of "I Miss You" by Blink-182 last Christmas, which went viral with over 1 million views. He personally replied to or liked every message he received. () Flying together with our sponsor Cyber Week = Even More Savings. For anyone still catching up on their holiday checklist (us too), to save the day: 11x14 Canvases are just $12.49 through December 6. You can also design custom photo books, ornaments, mugs, and over 50 same-day items—all ready in hours, not days. Perfect for last-minute gifting that still looks like you planned ahead. | Below are memories and traditions shared by some of our staff. ➤ Lights, boards, and togetherness: We drive around to look at Christmas lights, then come home and make themed charcuterie boards. Each board is made by one of my three adult children and their spouses or partners, plus one from my husband and me, so we end up with four or five different spreads. We play board games and end the night watching The Polar Express. Beth, The Flyover North Carolina writer ➤ A cake at midnight: My mother started a Christmas Eve tradition when the grandkids were old enough to understand what Christmas is really about. We bake a birthday cake for Jesus and make it the centerpiece of the table. At midnight, the youngest child blows out the candles after we all sing "Happy Birthday." It reminds us who we're celebrating. Afterward, the kids each open one gift. The tradition spread to family and friends around the country, and some years we had 50 or 60 friends, neighbors, and relatives gathered at midnight, many coming late just to celebrate Jesus' birthday. This year, the newest great-grandchild blew out the candles. Cathy, The Flyover Florida writer ➤ Back to the beginning: One winter break as a young man, I got The Lord of the Rings trilogy. I read all three straight through. When I finished, I read them backward, from the final chapter to the first, then read the entire trilogy again. Good times. David, editor-in-chief ➤ Saved for Christmas: Every Christmas for the last 40 years, our mom has made a much-anticipated dessert consisting of peppermint ice cream, hot fudge, peanuts, and a graham-cracker crust. She refuses to make it any other time of year. Chris, managing editor and occasional podcast host ➤ The longest wait: I remember going to church every Christmas Eve, which I loved, but my dad was the head deacon, so we always had to stay until every last dollar was counted and the door was locked before we could go home and open Christmas Eve presents. As soon as we got home, my siblings and I ripped off our church clothes, threw on pajamas, and raced to the tree, only to watch Dad take his sweet time. He'd make tea, change clothes, chat with Mom, maybe even make a sandwich, fully aware we were losing our minds. When he finally headed to the couch by the tree to start divvying up presents, he walked as slowly as possible with a big, mischievous smile. The three of us could barely hold it together, laughing and squirming with anticipation. I'll always remember that. Good things really do come to those who wait. Sarah, managing editor ➤ After Mass hunger: I remember, as a tween, desperately wanting to attend midnight Mass with my grandma, which meant sleeping over at her place with my cousin. My mom was a bit against it because she knew me. Still, after Christmas Eve dinner and presents, I headed to Grandma's. She took my cousin and me to midnight Mass, where I nearly fell asleep, my eyes swimming. But when we got home and Grandma went to bed, new life kicked in. Hungry, we made scrambled eggs. Grandma woke up and wandered into the kitchen in her nightgown, blinking at us like she couldn't quite believe what she was seeing. Scrambled eggs never tasted better. | | Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw
---
Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18]

clean_body: Starting as soon as a few months from now, the United States government will make a sweeping change to bank accounts nationwide. | | | Don't want to receive our publications? | | | Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18] | | | | | | Starting as soon as a few months from now, the United States government will make a sweeping change to bank accounts nationwide. | It will give them unprecedented powers to control your bank account. name: The PreBorn! Team
subject: She came looking for help You gave her hope
body:
Every day, women across America search online for answers.

| | | [](https://email.updates.conservativeinnovation.com/c/eJykUcuK3DAQ_Jr2bQa5ZUuTgw_DGodANuwpORpZ6tWI6IUkO-Tvg8PsF-yhoburqH7Ur9f1VTnvov2hAk36cGupa32QVoF8Sr9dtO-pPMjnv2m3KzIcLz1ecDx5MeVCWypx7TszaSM4djT1UgghBsZl95jkjW0oaONSKMMG2m6jQMkGLbXe0LDOTadmj2cwxvvrMCIq2Utm8Pbe8y8wsD0b1ahedYqVyqGaO8jFmM4sxatOofPTo7Vcgd8BF8DFuoOuz-1OAuDyrAAXlTMpX4EvVQOfX-7fe4bj1zdAoUIDPo8CUNgKfFYEKPYW1pr2ogn4nEuqmXRz0T6hQMbtAfhMQTn_bGoVsnI2Ap-18hSNKh9Iio3iOebtfnn5-e3y8YCuTCb_cd67KiQMzJ5y_69rn3HmmPBfAAAA__9S6aAV)
---
Hi Advocate for Life, Every day, women across America search online for answers. They are scared, uncertain, and convinced abortion is their only option. That's where **PreBorn!** steps in. Our Network Clinics across the nation provide **free ultrasounds** , **holistic** **compassionate care** , and the **gospel of Jesus Christ** to women in crisis. Because when a mother sees her baby for the first time, when she hears that heartbeat, everything changes. In fact, 81% of women who receive an ultrasound, can see their baby and hear the heartbeat, will choose life for their babies. And your **$28 gift provides one life-saving ultrasound** and gives her the chance to meet her baby and hear of God's love for her. As our thank-you, we'll send you PreBorn!'s [**_Tiny Miracles, Mighty Futures_ 2026**](https://email.updates.conservativeinnovation.com/c/eJykUcuK3DAQ_Jr2bQa5ZUuTgw_DGodANuwpORpZ6tWI6IUkO-Tvg8PsF-yhoburqH7Ur9f1VTnvov2hAk36cGupa32QVoF8Sr9dtO-pPMjnv2m3KzIcLz1ecDx5MeVCWypx7TszaSM4djT1UgghBsZl95jkjW0oaONSKMMG2m6jQMkGLbXe0LDOTadmj2cwxvvrMCIq2Utm8Pbe8y8wsD0b1ahedYqVyqGaO8jFmM4sxatOofPTo7Vcgd8BF8DFuoOuz-1OAuDyrAAXlTMpX4EvVQOfX-7fe4bj1zdAoUIDPo8CUNgKfFYEKPYW1pr2ogn4nEuqmXRz0T6hQMbtAfhMQTn_bGoVsnI2Ap-18hSNKh9Iio3iOebtfnn5-e3y8YCuTCb_cd67KiQMzJ5y_69rn3HmmPBfAAAA__9S6aAV) Calendar -- a celebration of the lives your gift helps save and the futures your faith helps build. Inside, you'll also find a **monthly prayer guide** to keep you connected to this mission all year long.

Our Network Clinics across the nation provide free ultrasounds , holistic compassionate care , and the gospel of Jesus Christ to women in crisis. Because when a mother sees her baby for the first time, when she hears that heartbeat, everything changes. In fact, 81% of women who receive an ultrasound, can see their baby and hear the heartbeat, will choose life for their babies. And your $28 gift provides one life-saving ultrasound and gives her the chance to meet her baby and hear of God's love for her. As our thank-you, we'll send you PreBorn!'s Calendar -- a celebration of the lives your gift helps save and the futures your faith helps build. Inside, you'll also find a monthly prayer guide to keep you connected to this mission all year long. Supplies are limited so give today to help reach the next mother before she's too late to hear her baby's heartbeat. Thank you for giving hope where it's needed most. Gratefully, The PreBorn! Team Pop Acta Media | Conservative Innovation PO Box 461 Fort Collins, CO 80522 | _

## Smart Freight Corridor Completed
A 21-mile stretch of State Highway 130 from Georgetown to Mustang Ridge has been [designated](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0LFugzAQgOGngc3IPmwTBg9d2NK1IzL2EawYjOxrSN6-ImqfoMNNJ_26776u49WGGLbbp13R0HNO44ZHiUiEeSyrzTRnDLeFXMo5-JRdOqY0AgfFBDBQo7chvkZZezNx34sajei01loK3taL8ZMSfSulhR6Ug7lDrrXTTnRKe-XaOpgzJuAcLiQ0WlwUoGi1mzp56aGSHH2glBtakPBpyxxf6YG5cWmto1mI9lK1HxUMFQzHcTT3p93OZQXDqalgKAsTLS_sLWK_JPZnYmFje7QOGSW2YNyZz-GBOWIprOAaGOVvdz9DdTZ-P0KMoeiukvy22hDfh9D_3vcw8BMAAP__xkKFhw) the "Smart Freight Corridor." Over the past two years, the nation's fastest highway has been [equipped](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkDuS5CAMhk-DM7pA-BkQbELWm27owiC3qcWmS6hft5_yBHOBCRTpq__17zpffcrpuP31O1p-r2U-8FUzMiPNdffEK2G6bRwKUYqFQnktZQYFndQgoZujT_kzt020i4qTbtDqoe_7vtXKNJv1Bpd10gOsox7NFJQ3aIIaQ2dUF_3aJHuKaThP6RYuvR47QG36sAztOIFoFcbEhS68IePb1zV_yhPpEsreZLsx36swfwQ4Aa7eMTA99lyCz2eXkxLg-C3A-UfldAhw50OAO50FOA0CnOp-AOkJveQiaymHrIgyUnoiZaxVVtyTZHqE_7UhG--vlHOq_SBaddt9yt-p-HdbPi18BQAA__9i84kk) with sensors, cameras, and radars that monitor traffic conditions and relay that information to autonomous vehicles traveling the route. TxDOT partnered with infrastructure technology company Cavnue on the first-of-its-kind project. Approximately 57,000 vehicles travel SH 130 daily, and a statement from the company indicated that officials are considering [expanding the technology](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykULFuhDAU-xrYgpJHCDBk6MJ2XTuiQB7HUwNBybvj7u8rOvQHOliyLMuy_XUbb44C7fdPt6Hl1xLHHc8ckBnTmDeXeElI95XnmBL5mOZ4TnEECY1QIKAZvaPwHnXp7SR9r0q0qjXGGK1kXa7W9D0sCIC16QA0Gjm1utZu0UY1rvcl2StMwQWpNFRGdQ2gqs08tbrrodASPXFMFa_I-HJ5Ce_4xFTNcSuDXZmPXNQfBQwFDOd5Vts7u93tHHeKl6mA4VpVwOAemWm_SGKaA_5Jwid6YgqYs-D0mL-zyKtQtRSgAKSBrjrWo0zWHyeFQNm0hZb3zVH4rcH_O-9p4ScAAP__UNmDdw) to portions of I-10, I-35, and I-45, "strengthening freight movement within the Texas Triangle." Earlier this year, legislators [passed a law](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLGu6yAMQL-GbFTgEJIMDG_Jdt_6xoiA0yARqMBt2r9_or9wB0sejo6P_O9n_bEhhnT_a0809N7zmvCqEYmwrPW0hfaC4X6Qy6UEn4vL15ZXEDBwCRyG1dsQP6vqvNmEn2WHRo5aa62k6LvDyNHaybvBar3Ndu_tKGblexT9pvZtgC6YJpPQRkgFNy2nAVD22m2jmmZgSqAPlMuNDiR827rHT35hubl8dtEcRI_K-j8MFgYLHfgtamBqBIOlHWCwiKntY4OahVPmmPZcHPKEF7dPyimf-Vn5C4_gIvLyjFi5PYPn1e5IH-5yclhSZbB0xfjHFWIMVY9MiftpQ_w20e8--TLwPwAA__-SZoj4) requiring businesses operating autonomous vehicles to obtain authorization from the Texas Department of Motor Vehicles.
## Cowboys vs. Commanders
The Dallas Cowboys [visit](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL1urDAQhZ8GOiN7MAYKF7eh29umRAYPMJLBK3uyLG8fscobpDjdp_P39RgfjgId63-3o-X3EscDzxyQGdOYd5d4SUjrxnNMiXxMczynOIKERigQ0IzeUbhGXXo7Sd-rEq1qjTFGK1mXm0WcnPRGGr9o1fQGGtXL3nRt0-neybYke5spuCWVhsqorgFUtZmnVnc9FFqiJ46p4g0Z3y4v4YovTNUc9zLYjfmZi_pfAUMBw3melXchuPxpeuWbKmC4ZxUwTLSKJ838nVAoKX4ZMcd9d4fHlEXmmK5AB2bxrjujymT986QQKJu20HLdHYVPNP_tsJeFnwAAAP__rZKAlA) the Washington Commanders in the first of three NFL division rivalry games today. The game will be broadcast on Netflix at noon. The Cowboys (6-8-1) and Commanders (4-11) have both been eliminated from playoff contention. Dallas head coach Brian Schottenheimer indicated Monday he plans to start highly-paid stars Dak Prescott and CeeDee Lamb, [dismissing concerns](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkDGOpDAQRU8DmVu42thN4GATstl0Q-TGBZTW2C1XaTx9-xWjvcEEFdT_0tPT__OxfARKlPff4UQvX1tZMjZOKIJ14TNU2SrSfshaaqVY6lrasywwwKg0KBiXGCi9F9NH_xzipHv02llrrdHDvT98XB3q-zi5OLohmOhsfICxAM5Obp2mnvwF03DdoA3crH6MgPpu16czjwk6M2AkKfUmBwp-Bd7Su3xiva3l7JM_RF7c3X91MHcwt9ZuTFfVwZy31MH8rfzmDuaSma4Y2_U9K4WseD2KCOYD6cSqIvFJzMiqEv9VrxTelHcVQ0qB1X-UYglVsLLaKIekpBW1hxO5rz6-GqVEbF1nhv0MlL495Wfrfnr4FwAA__8NRJH9) about injury risks. "_We play a physical game_ ," said Schottenheimer. "_Everybody understands that. These guys, when they go out, they don’t think that way. They play_." Last week, Lamb [tied Michael Irvin](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLGO5CAMhp-GdIzAYQIpKK5Jt9deGUFwJj6RMDLczObtT1mtdA9whWVLtn9__n99zB-BMh2Pn2FH3z7XMh_4rhlbQ57rHritjPTY2lKYKRVeyjuWGRTcpQYJ9zkFyudsuuSjSqPu0Gs7DMNgtOq7zS-gxx5cDDoqABeds9oahNGsMd2d68hfYhquUNrAbdDuDqj7YYnWuBGEUZioFb61DRt-hrrms7yQb0vZu-y31p5V9D8ETAKmL76zvonx9qeGVlI4r0EBU22Fzys_C7cqYDrW_G9DwHSBCJg0XLW-WogJUeawR_m70FHlTssWMEviFx2SDsmBUX5LyIbM9H3FWWd7NfZKWQFTxz4935Qz1cEKox57oPz1QPs_218e_gYAAP__H6-VvA) in accumulating at least 1,000 receiving yards for five consecutive seasons, and Prescott is chasing the league lead in passing yards. The Cowboys will [debut a uniform combination](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL2u4yAQhZ_G7rAAY-wUFNu4y7ZbWhgGeyR-skBC8vZXzivcYjTFOfr06fy7b3eNHuPxVwdQ9e3SFqEVD7VC3krQuboMeJzVpJzRpmxS29PGKZ8I44RPm9XoP5vordqpvbEeFJullFIwOvanmsdFODcB3cUMzhi6jOC4kG6f7WwW3aO6YIxfR5ngg2TLxIGN0uyzWG68ExQs1pSHekKFty7Of9IL8mBS6L06a32UbvzT8bXja2ttKHhFHV-j8x1fv8qfQhp6TxroTJ4R_z_hei7lQEwKO0ZdMUVizoylBl3IoQMQfWiMpV6VoKOFXPqs7ONCYZFzJ-gRNPqvSv3dgC_FfwIAAP__0C2I_w) for the first time—arctic white helmets, navy blue jerseys, and white pants. Fort Worth native Kelly Clarkson will [kick things off](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0LGOtCAUBeCnwQ4DqCgFxd_Yzd9uaRi46s0gTODuOPP2G_cVtjjdyZeT83Vbbg4jpu2_O8DSe81LgrNGIIKy1MMVWgvgtpPPpWDIxefznhcl1MCl4mpYgsP4Wfom2LsIRjZg5ai11r0UXbNbY0B76fXadcINxvgp9JMejZ-8DwCyQXthUl0RsletltOgQHba38d-Mor1AgJSLi3tQPB2dY2f_ILS-nw00e5Ez8q6f0zNTM3nebZbzmHP3xUeAE9M21VkaoZEUMhhOiARUzO9eN3zWZmanTZjJ6ZBMjU_IMYP99GVR82J-71gpcNVvrkDKk9r5AlojfjmmCq5rbhLb4oNzxNjxKpH1ovtcBh_J9Lfjn1Z9RMAAP__1kuPqw) with a performance of her hit song _Underneath the Tree_.

**_Thanks to all of you who submitted your Christmas memories. These are a few of our favorites._** **➤ Georgia (Alleyton):** My most memorable Christmas was in 1948. That year, I asked Santa for a bicycle and a doll the size of a real child. On Christmas morning, we ran into the living room to see what Santa had brought us. There was my brand-new bike with a life-sized boy doll sitting on it like he was riding it! But Daddy said, "Wait, I think I heard something on the roof!" So he led us out the front door, and we found presents Santa had dropped. It was magical! Daddy always made Christmas special for my sister and me, and he always taught us to remember the reason we celebrate and exchange gifts. **➤ Chuck (Richmond):** I grew up in northeast Ohio. When I was a kid, we went to bed on Christmas Eve with nothing in the house. Overnight, Santa came and put up the tree and ornaments, filled the stockings, decorated the house, and put all the presents under the tree. I always wondered why my parents were so tired on Christmas Day. **➤ Jimmey McLemore (Southeast Texas):** When I was in the third grade, we celebrated Christmas at our new house in Jasper, which was still under construction. It had outside walls, windows, and electricity, but it was only partially insulated and had no running water. Still, my dad was determined to spend Christmas in the house. After opening presents on Christmas morning, my mom made scrambled eggs and sausage with an electric skillet. We used two rolls of insulation and a sheet of plywood to make a table. That was 1984. Every Christmas since then, we've had scrambled eggs with chopped sausage for breakfast on Christmas morning. I'm married and have a family of my own now, and we still have the same breakfast every Christmas. **➤ Ken Marx (Fort Worth):** My most memorable Christmas Eve was in 1999, when I visited Israel as a member of the Southwest Baptist Seminary Oratorio Chorus. That evening, we performed at a Baptist Church in Bethlehem and at the Jerusalem Civic Auditorium. Those performances were the culmination of a week of visiting many places where Jesus walked. I have never had another Yuletide like it. **➤ Annette (Cedar Hill):** The Christmas (mis)adventure I remember most vividly occurred in the late 1940s when I was four years old. Our Christmas tree was decorated with lovely, bubbling lights and other glass ornaments. I’m sure I tried to leave the tree alone, but those lights were beguiling. So, when my mother was busy elsewhere, I pulled a chair over to the tree and climbed up to get a closer look. The chair and I toppled into the tree, knocking it over and breaking more than a few of the ornaments. My mother's reaction was predictable. I did not enjoy that prolonged timeout. **➤ Susan (Lubbock):** My mother was born during the Great Depression and lived through World War II. As a result, she learned to be frugal, to the point that she would carefully open Christmas gifts so that she could save and reuse the wrapping paper. When my dad was between jobs one year, she couldn't buy a Christmas tree, but she was determined to make the holiday special for us children. She gathered tumbleweeds, sprayed them green, and used the carefully preserved trimmings (including tinsel icicles) to turn them into a lovely "tree." Under the tree were a few carefully selected gifts wrapped in that saved paper. She sewed new stockings using scraps of muslin, green burlap, and red rickrack. On Christmas morning, those stockings were filled with an apple, an orange, nuts, and a bundle of old-fashioned ribbon candy. There was also an inexpensive necklace for me and a toy car for each of my brothers.
