On , the Houston and Texas Central Railroad reached Austin. Within the next five years, the capital city's population doubled. Merry Christmas! Today is the one day of the year when our whole team takes the day off, so there'll be no Texas Flyover tomorrow. Our weekend editions will also be a bit shorter, allowing our staff to spend time with their families. The full Texas Flyover will return on Monday. Today’s sponsor, , is highlighting a simple way Amazon Prime shoppers can earn serious cash back and score a valuable Amazon bonus—just for getting approved, with no extra spending required. Take** The Flyoverwith you. Busy day? Listen to our daily 15-minute podcast while commuting, cooking, or walking the dog—your Flyover fix, anywhere. | | Smart Freight Corridor Completed A 21-mile stretch of State Highway 130 from Georgetown to Mustang Ridge has been the "Smart Freight Corridor." Over the past two years, the nation's fastest highway has been with sensors, cameras, and radars that monitor traffic conditions and relay that information to autonomous vehicles traveling the route. TxDOT partnered with infrastructure technology company Cavnue on the first-of-its-kind project. Approximately 57,000 vehicles travel SH 130 daily, and a statement from the company indicated that officials are considering to portions of I-10, I-35, and I-45, "strengthening freight movement within the Texas Triangle." Earlier this year, legislators requiring businesses operating autonomous vehicles to obtain authorization from the Texas Department of Motor Vehicles. Cowboys vs. Commanders The Dallas Cowboys the Washington Commanders in the first of three NFL division rivalry games today. The game will be broadcast on Netflix at noon. The Cowboys (6-8-1) and Commanders (4-11) have both been eliminated from playoff contention. Dallas head coach Brian Schottenheimer indicated Monday he plans to start highly-paid stars Dak Prescott and CeeDee Lamb, about injury risks. "We play a physical game ," said Schottenheimer. "Everybody understands that. These guys, when they go out, they don’t think that way. They play." Last week, Lamb in accumulating at least 1,000 receiving yards for five consecutive seasons, and Prescott is chasing the league lead in passing yards. The Cowboys will for the first time—arctic white helmets, navy blue jerseys, and white pants. Fort Worth native Kelly Clarkson will with a performance of her hit song Underneath the Tree. | Flying together with our sponsor If you spend a good amount on Amazon , . Even better, you could get approved extremely fast—and if you are, you’ll receive an insanely valuable welcome bonus deposited straight into your Amazon account for immediate use. No hoops or extra spending. . If you shop at Amazon or Whole Foods, this card could help you earn meaningful cash back from purchases you’re already making. This offer won’t last forever, and for Prime members, it’s basically a no-brainer. Amazon Prime members: | Thanks to all of you who submitted your Christmas memories. These are a few of our favorites. ➤ Georgia (Alleyton): My most memorable Christmas was in 1948. That year, I asked Santa for a bicycle and a doll the size of a real child. On Christmas morning, we ran into the living room to see what Santa had brought us. There was my brand-new bike with a life-sized boy doll sitting on it like he was riding it! But Daddy said, "Wait, I think I heard something on the roof!" So he led us out the front door, and we found presents Santa had dropped. It was magical! Daddy always made Christmas special for my sister and me, and he always taught us to remember the reason we celebrate and exchange gifts. ➤ Chuck (Richmond): I grew up in northeast Ohio. When I was a kid, we went to bed on Christmas Eve with nothing in the house. Overnight, Santa came and put up the tree and ornaments, filled the stockings, decorated the house, and put all the presents under the tree. I always wondered why my parents were so tired on Christmas Day. ➤ Jimmey McLemore (Southeast Texas): When I was in the third grade, we celebrated Christmas at our new house in Jasper, which was still under construction. It had outside walls, windows, and electricity, but it was only partially insulated and had no running water. Still, my dad was determined to spend Christmas in the house. After opening presents on Christmas morning, my mom made scrambled eggs and sausage with an electric skillet. We used two rolls of insulation and a sheet of plywood to make a table. That was 1984. Every Christmas since then, we've had scrambled eggs with chopped sausage for breakfast on Christmas morning. I'm married and have a family of my own now, and we still have the same breakfast every Christmas. ➤ Ken Marx (Fort Worth): My most memorable Christmas Eve was in 1999, when I visited Israel as a member of the Southwest Baptist Seminary Oratorio Chorus. That evening, we performed at a Baptist Church in Bethlehem and at the Jerusalem Civic Auditorium. Those performances were the culmination of a week of visiting many places where Jesus walked. I have never had another Yuletide like it. ➤ Annette (Cedar Hill): The Christmas (mis)adventure I remember most vividly occurred in the late 1940s when I was four years old. Our Christmas tree was decorated with lovely, bubbling lights and other glass ornaments. I’m sure I tried to leave the tree alone, but those lights were beguiling. So, when my mother was busy elsewhere, I pulled a chair over to the tree and climbed up to get a closer look. The chair and I toppled into the tree, knocking it over and breaking more than a few of the ornaments. My mother's reaction was predictable. I did not enjoy that prolonged timeout. ➤ Susan (Lubbock): My mother was born during the Great Depression and lived through World War II. As a result, she learned to be frugal, to the point that she would carefully open Christmas gifts so that she could save and reuse the wrapping paper. When my dad was between jobs one year, she couldn't buy a Christmas tree, but she was determined to make the holiday special for us children. She gathered tumbleweeds, sprayed them green, and used the carefully preserved trimmings (including tinsel icicles) to turn them into a lovely "tree." Under the tree were a few carefully selected gifts wrapped in that saved paper. She sewed new stockings using scraps of muslin, green burlap, and red rickrack. On Christmas morning, those stockings were filled with an apple, an orange, nuts, and a bundle of old-fashioned ribbon candy. There was also an inexpensive necklace for me and a toy car for each of my brothers. I was just old enough to remember and appreciate what she did for the family and how she made Christmas morning magical. subject: Maine GOP Files FOAA for Fraud Report Data Amid No-Bid Contract Spending and Welfare Fraud Allegations
body:

** Maine GOP Files FOAA for Fraud Report Data Amid No-Bid Contract Spending and Welfare Fraud Allegations (https://mainepolicy.us9.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a975bf67c40a8d8be91ad606d&id=2850acab79&e=f093fa355c)
------------------------------------------------------------
By Seamus Othot on Dec 24, 2025 04:33 pm
As Gov. Janet Mills’ (D-Maine) administration has blown millions on no-bid contracts, and allegations of Medicaid fraud have touched upon sitting Democratic state representatives, the Maine GOP filed a records request for the number of fraud reports received and investigated by the Office of the State Auditor. ------------------------------------------------------------
By Jon Fetherston on Dec 24, 2025 04:22 pm
SCARBOROUGH, Maine — Scarborough firefighters were called to the scene of a serious motor vehicle crash Friday afternoon that required extrication at the intersection of Haigis Parkway and Payne Road, according to a post from the Scarborough Professional Fire Fighters. Multiple Scarborough agencies responded to the collision, with Saco crews providing mutual aid along with [...]

** They Break It, Others Rewrite It: How The Maine Wire Became Maine’s Agenda-Setter (https://mainepolicy.us9.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a975bf67c40a8d8be91ad606d&id=da77911ddf&e=f093fa355c)
------------------------------------------------------------
By Jon Fetherston on Dec 24, 2025 04:16 pm

| | **Real Autonomous Power Hits The Screens**
---
| [ Caught On Camera ](https://links.main.yourcapitalminds.com/u/click?_t=affa6bfca09a497f86454ba803e6a9a2&_m=c3221f5e8f8440b5b109351243e43879&_e=1b9gr2Q7XiMuxzijHt3irQ4lNz64k5-9g3lCvgKh2CHpdq71PNngFhZNZNHpi4MFtXBH8Dlk5UzR8V2-J1oAwYkUl-uQjEuky9bXGInIxq-H4IQsvJPl1JGb5EFQb4Uee5Uy5CIKYEi3TCSyptPKplrd4aQ66YWNruehGb-aMJuT6I7YXERNsDngekwCDM1WGP7oyIqCoz6u9eBRiSmRhBz0LTBrZkIqPknG4nPnluNefoyRoJRuRGB2Nhb8Q-nvAUoS6Fl7mihyrt2Jn5mU_7u_qXy5HQOyme-_-dZ3_IqlvOaY_RfGmzJ1dha7pqkJemGFf84xQC0D_vg0Xumf8JDTSZLXAMhRdWIzSi20Gc-9O_afQtcWZfASDCIOp72YS62R5w24d1SMUZ_2R-9-puCni9IBSn4__rDxW7lnhs3AVPS_dDynjmL8WdGVuGNIIdLi_9iff3pWDyrbkpCzMMbrg8FIbiMeV0X7paXmeZ0tdVpA6hoh7Jnsw-DsePl57rGcIa6quxT_sD99-dJtTd4y5uI0gLaGAis43njUks31HdmwzFx_bH3Uh1svnhBw3IGXAjvhiVafVKVZ3L2Ugw%3D%3D)
---
| | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.
---

[Watch the panic-inducing reveal now](https://links.main.yourcapitalminds.com/u/click?_t=affa6bfca09a497f86454ba803e6a9a2&_m=c3221f5e8f8440b5b109351243e43879&_e=1b9gr2Q7XiMuxzijHt3irQ4lNz64k5-9g3lCvgKh2CHpdq71PNngFhZNZNHpi4MFtXBH8Dlk5UzR8V2-J1oAwYkUl-uQjEuky9bXGInIxq-H4IQsvJPl1JGb5EFQb4Uee5Uy5CIKYEi3TCSyptPKplrd4aQ66YWNruehGb-aMJuT6I7YXERNsDngekwCDM1WkPsKRqfYpL0km1IzGp4jFV7bxgChG5ES4SzwlLXMprpqxszffHSKB29NZHjY8PjWaZ5FusWw20l_bw-NF9LpFGPYRIkBFgfcRKSplFjgC4m5OlQDGysl8yX8o7ETDn9pv2ok1Ws7ElMvsFiIEnPfCTTJH7FjbT5wkrDR-rYC3DXuqXqYLRhyUxyF_DfL7nDwGaM2sl_PE0XRS5SfbmX6lcyqdVcdFDMGOG-uLYsYXKXYRobmBr4btCNN87t3PcvGeDiNKdkO3Qeg9PU2ZRsre1EOKSjz9U5BwiPNVCXqP0G5Yk5sz3ZFh28Npbm6IEhAiq99n41S3PFd5XcN6iNs3P4O72d1x58HbPlFyiwzV8vKwDTTlrshZQUtl7X2mQxtmt7Qd8N2HbfNw9rvktrd0g%3D%3D)
Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671.

The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. name: Governor Kelly Ayotte
subject: Merry Christmas!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kelly for New Hampshire, Chris Connelly Treasurer, PO Box 4723 Manchester, NH 03108
body:
Wishing everyone a happy holiday season with the blessings of family, friends, and freedom.

| | | | | |
---
| | | | | | Friend,
Merry Christmas! Whether you’re home for the holidays or traveling, Joe, Kate, Jake, and I hope you are having a wonderful day celebrating with family and friends.
We are so grateful for all of your support and encouragement over the last year. We wish you and your loved ones a joyful holiday season filled with the blessings of family, friends, and freedom.
Today, we also remember the men and women of our armed forces, law enforcement, and first responders who are away from their families keeping our communities, our state, and our nation safe. I am grateful to each and every one of them for their service.
Thank you again for your support — I’m honored to have the privilege of serving as your Governor, and I am ready for another great year of keeping New Hampshire safe, prosperous and free in 2026!
Wishing you a very Merry Christmas and a Happy New Year!
Best wishes,
Kelly
---

name: Ted Cruz
subject: Christ Is Born — Rejoicing With You Today
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Ted Cruz for Senate
body:
Wishing you a quiet, Holy, and hope-filled Christmas Day.

| | |
---
| | Fellow Conservative, **I just wanted to send you a note to wish you a very _Merry Christmas!_** Today reminds me of how deeply God loves us, enough to come near, to walk with us, and to bring His light into our everyday lives. I'm grateful for that hope, and I'm grateful for you. I pray your day is filled with peace, joy, and the comfort of Christ. _May God bless your home, your heart, and the days ahead with His grace!_ **_Merry Christmas!_** Ted Cruz

name: Mike Rogers
subject: Wishing You a Merry Christmas!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Rogers for Senate
body:
Warm holiday wishes as we reflect on gratitude, community, and hope.

| | |
---
| | [](https://links.rogersforsenate.com/u/click?_t=293de5a6223e4436ab53fb8020578890&_m=e4ef3da1785b4a088b450e77eff9fa32&_e=oWB_d4O1c-u8Q46qjm5tNIcLbO5pJKAa4H9nIHyawS4pQ74NQt_eXHqEULGm48RboMAAtaiWOJUt43_DLpAwnCNff8aBbs6WOlq97egE5YGcE-UN0wGYb62jqQx70-8Fghkhqdn1H7UTwf_mM4j6i0boH8FF88gHpj-EaMIQ8ZaafSaUBcakrjtp29fB9Ky57B3IeyXxbPyPe7T0bOEsFjq-4SBPHrr7KGz5A4Ok1xTUscdEjYHvs5Z6PN6cy86Avl7iFLVYtRHEnpzxCUpEtEJb-m2hu0kyfIic_FT0L_iZ3RhbaeV0VN2Q5yX3BhSb1678_kA5tlyvkpEGHL7IrIlAJSOkZChwT5i69pQshgjIdWu41BkFUa4iYwiLKfLlLay_uMzco2y1a9ptpkgQaDXHXr0mXnhrPLQ7KDaM9zp_UEl1j2VHqyU-HzlVAsrl6XSgCQZzTA12qFWP4rl2dc-EopjRfeFiMWsMWjdx4ZsHiijUNrHey8jSoaOO4hWVAptlOh1W7-lCvEnl-ODQVLUNtZDslcU1Fz0wseLLd0ZBmsQbTorEDTpG7lt3xXmvaIQqk5xpQjakuIiAdS-b2wbK_8g9__ZTpFCgHPtfSlwE0vFyMV8QmO3MXmABOBQmpC023vgg11GmlT5iYTBQXvlDCWjNTa2YSlrNEpBokFonqC_pyb-Mx4Sp6_4zg1Px2FTurIkLe_jiUzEpfejCsT8CP8H3sc5LU9kbg6elByBR6xx8IPNgXs02Bn3p6YB8PQMeo4quD7sGDt6egeWwNEaVBz814e6PncArT-fXBHnUbtd1vf6WXbBa6lLUzhWXHLpmXC98pPnXM0WyycKU7isTQR1XbkF84pPrueGczEI%3D)Dear Friend, As Christmas arrives, I want to take a moment to wish you and your family a joyful and peaceful holiday.

This season invites us to slow down, gather with the people we love, and reflect on the blessings we sometimes take for granted – the strength of our communities, the kindness of neighbors, and the enduring spirit that defines Michigan and our nation.

I’m grateful for the encouragement and goodwill you share throughout the year. It’s a reminder that even in challenging times, decency, service, and hope remain at the heart of who we are.

May your Christmas be filled with warmth, laughter, and meaningful moments with those closest to you.

Merry Christmas and best wishes,

Mike Rogers**
**

name: Erin Houchin
subject: Merry Christmas & Happy New Year!
body:
Merry Christmas! As we celebrate the Christmas season, I pray your home is filled with joy, peace, and the people you love.

| | | | | [|
---](https://erinhouchin.us22.list-manage.com/track/click?u=060500d14b42b6eab7cf78f6a&id=5dbf66b8b6&e=203c27af0d)
| | | | | | | | | | | |
Merry Christmas!
As we celebrate the Christmas season, I pray your home is filled with joy, peace, and the people you love.
From the Houchin family to yours, we wish you a Christmas day full of blessings and memories that carry into the year ahead.
---
[|
---](https://erinhouchin.us22.list-manage.com/track/click?u=060500d14b42b6eab7cf78f6a&id=0502e242c2&e=203c27af0d)
| |
This season gives us a chance to slow down and reflect, and I’m grateful for the opportunity to serve and represent Indiana’s Ninth District in Congress. Your encouragement and support over the past year has meant so much.
May this season bring you rest, renewed hope, and a renewed sense of why we celebrate.
John 1:14 reminds us of how the birth of Jesus changed everything: _“And the Word became flesh and dwelt among us, full of grace and truth.”_
Sincerely,
**Erin Houchin**
---
| | | | | | | | | [](https://erinhouchin.us22.list-manage.com/track/click?u=060500d14b42b6eab7cf78f6a&id=b54aa86fcd&e=203c27af0d)

name: Tim Walz
subject: Wishing you a wonderful holiday season
disclaimer_text: paid for by Walz for Governor
body:
# [](https://act.timwalz.com/go/64?t=1&akid=346%2E34582%2ExyNZIT)
Peter, on behalf of my family and me, I want to wish you and yours a Merry Christmas and a happy holiday season!
Thank you for all of your support and dedication this year. This work isn’t always easy, but we must continue to join together to fight for our future.
However you celebrate, I hope you’re able to join with family and friends for the most magical time of the year.
Happy holidays, Peter.
— Tim Walz

Email us: [info@walzforgovernor.org](mailto:info@walzforgovernor.org)
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way we keep in touch with people like you, so thank you for reading to the end.

name: Josh Hawley
subject: For to us a child is born
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Josh Hawley Victory Committee
body:
| | |
---
| |
---
Gifts, family, hot chocolate by the fire — it's the Christmas season! But when we peel away how the world wants to define Christmas, we are left with a single truth: the Savior of the world was born in a manger, and the salvation of the world was underway!
---
---
| | As we spend time with family, enjoying all that God has blessed us with, we look back to the Prophet Isaiah who proclaimed a hope to come. A hope that has come
_For to us a child is born,_
_to us a son is given,_
_and the government will be on his shoulders._
_And he will be called_
_Wonderful Counselor, Mighty God,_
_Everlasting Father, Prince of Peace._
_Of the greatness of his government and peace_
_there will be no end._
**Isaiah 9:6-7**
---
Merry Christmas,
---
---
Josh Hawley
---

---

name: Freedom First Project

We're putting together a Christmas card signed by President Trump's most loyal America First supporters - and space is limited. Congresswoman Monica De La Cruz has already signed her name. Now, she's inviting YOU to join her. If you want your name included on the front page of the card, please don't wait. CHRISTMAS, PRESIDENT TRUMPThis Christmas season, we thank you for your leadership, your strength, and your unwavering commitment to the American people. Your courage and conviction have inspired millions - and this holiday, we celebrate Faith, Freedom, and the values that make our nation great. body:
Peter,
We don't usually write emails like this.
But when Speaker Mike Johnson emails you... and Newt Gingrich follows up too...
That's a sign that something is wrong. What's happening in South Texas is DIRE.
National Democrats are zeroing in on this race. Outside money is lining up. And the margin for error is disappearing fast.
We've pulled every lever, we've sounded every alarm, and we're running out of options.

This isn't about hype or headlines. It's about whether we stop the Left here - or give them an opening they've been waiting for.

What's happening in South Texas is DIRE. National Democrats are zeroing in on this race. Outside money is lining up. And the margin for error is disappearing fast. We've pulled every lever, we've sounded every alarm, and we're running out of options. This isn't about hype or headlines. It's about whether we stop the Left here - or give them an opening they've been waiting for. wouldn't ask if this weren't serious. Thank you for standing with us when it counts. -Team Monica * Democrats already pledged up to FIFTY MILLION dollars to wipe me out for being a MAGA Latina in a purple district... And that was all BEFORE I was added to this new target list! name: Tracey Mann
subject: Merry Christmas!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Mann for Congress, Inc
body:
This is a special time to pause, reflect, and give thanks for the many blessings God has given us — especially faith, family, and community. | | | [ ](https://traceymann.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=60a16ea89a176679a5f31366c&id=14f26e6d18&e=a9d1410029)
---
| |
---
| From our family to yours, we wish you a Merry Christmas.

As we celebrate this holy season, we pray that Christmas brings you peace, joy, and time to gather with those you love. This is a special time to pause, reflect, and give thanks for the many blessings God has given us — especially faith, family, and community.

Scripture reminds us of the true meaning of Christmas:

“For unto us a child is born, unto us a son is given: and the government shall be upon his shoulder: and his name shall be called Wonderful, Counsellor, The mighty God, The everlasting Father, The Prince of Peace.”
— Isaiah 9:6

We are grateful for the opportunity to serve and for the trust you place in us. May the hope of Christ’s birth fill your heart and your home this Christmas and throughout the coming year.

Merry Christmas, and God bless.

Tracey Mann

name: Wes Moore
subject: Happy holidays from my family to yours!
body:
# [](https://act.wesmoore.com/go/8?t=1&akid=1105%2E1086043%2EgeruIg)
**Peter, from my family to yours, I want to wish you a wonderful holiday season**. I hope you’re able to enjoy this time of family, joy, and reflection with the ones you love.
A few weeks ago, Dawn and I welcomed over 800 Marylanders to the People’s House for our annual Holiday Open House. It’s one of my favorite parts of being governor. Meeting with you all and hearing your stories from the past year. _I’m excited to hear from even more of you as we hit the campaign trail in 2026._
I’m off to spend this Christmas Day with Dawn, Mia, and James, but I wanted to make sure to _thank you_ for your continued support. It means the world to all of us.
Happy holidays,
Wes
_PS. Enjoy this photo of Tucker in a Santa hat._

* * *
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wes-em-footer?refcode=em-20251225&t=2&refcode2=1105_1086043_geruIg&akid=1105%2E1086043%2EgeruIg)**
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:

Email us: [info@wesmoore.com](mailto:info@wesmoore.com)
BY AUTHORITY: WES MOORE FOR MARYLAND, MARY TYDINGS, TREASURER

name: Lt. Gen. Michael S. Linnington, USO President and CEO
subject: A very merry Christmas from the USO
body:
Thank you for supporting our service members.
[](https://www.uso.org)
[](https://links.e.uso.org/els/v2/A-VKBbX9pPfL7/Y1NMamxhOXA3VWd5WlVlSWhjbGhWR3h0UWRBVVlpS1A5dzRhZzFVa2pkY1A3T3UweGxlVGxBUXRjbU1tZHc2bEpVem5JMWQ3dk9tVm1RcUVGQ3ZKRUsyQS9XS1N0dndHZjFOaGI4WmdJZUU9S0/NGRXWGhwbzcyZFBuZFc3NFQ1MXk0K244dEw4UFAwYU5XZzhjS2xKQTNzMm1BQmduamdTNksycm9xOCs2b004Ym9GNWtaK3k2c2ZzaXl0bm82VmZ3NHFPaW9Xa0RlbVZFS0)
---
Dear USO Supporter,
I want to take this opportunity to wish you and your family a very merry Christmas. As the new CEO of the USO and as a retired 35-year solider, I am eternally grateful for the commitment, dedication and care you've shown to our service members. They're the backbone of our nation, and their service and sacrifice are why we can all celebrate freely today with our loved ones.
Our military service members faced great challenges this year: international tension, a government shutdown that put strain on tens of thousands of families, recovery efforts for natural disasters, deployments to areas they've never been to and time away from their families. Even when they're home, our heroes are always on standby, ready to drop everything for us.
At the USO, we take great pride in supporting our soldiers. We are there for them and their families, _especially_ during the holidays. This is why I'm thankful for _you_ today. You help us ensure that no soldier feels forgotten during Christmas -- whether through a card to uplift them, a warm meal to remind them of home or internet access so they can call their families. **You help us make the difference for them.**
[SUPPORT OUR SERVICE MEMBERS](https://links.e.uso.org/els/v2/Dd9Kh-2gVzQW/Y1NMamxhOXA3VWd5WlVlSWhjbGhWR3h0UWRBVVlpS1A5dzRhZzFVa2pkY1A3T3UweGxlVGxBUXRjbU1tZHc2bEpVem5JMWQ3dk9tVm1RcUVGQ3ZKRUsyQS9XS1N0dndHZjFOaGI4WmdJZUU9S0/NGRXWGhwbzcyZFBuZFc3NFQ1MXk0K244dEw4UFAwYU5XZzhjS2xKQTNzMm1BQmduamdTNksycm9xOCs2b004Ym9GNWtaK3k2c2ZzaXl0bm82VmZ3NHFPaW9Xa0RlbVZFS0)
---
From all of us at the USO, thank you for the care you've shown our heroes. We wish you and your loved ones a very happy holiday season.
For the People Who Serve,
[](https://links.e.uso.org/els/v2/mWbZT-dem4QA/Y1NMamxhOXA3VWd5WlVlSWhjbGhWR3h0UWRBVVlpS1A5dzRhZzFVa2pkY1A3T3UweGxlVGxBUXRjbU1tZHc2bEpVem5JMWQ3dk9tVm1RcUVGQ3ZKRUsyQS9XS1N0dndHZjFOaGI4WmdJZUU9S0/NGRXWGhwbzcyZFBuZFc3NFQ1MXk0K244dEw4UFAwYU5XZzhjS2xKQTNzMm1BQmduamdTNksycm9xOCs2b004Ym9GNWtaK3k2c2ZzaXl0bm82VmZ3NHFPaW9Xa0RlbVZFS0)
---

clean_body: Thank you for supporting our service members. Dear USO Supporter, I want to take this opportunity to wish you and your family a very merry Christmas. As the new CEO of the USO and as a retired 35-year solider, I am eternally grateful for the commitment, dedication and care you've shown to our service members. They're the backbone of our nation, and their service and sacrifice are why we can all celebrate freely today with our loved ones. Our military service members faced great challenges this year: international tension, a government shutdown that put strain on tens of thousands of families, recovery efforts for natural disasters, deployments to areas they've never been to and time away from their families. Even when they're home, our heroes are always on standby, ready to drop everything for us. At the USO, we take great pride in supporting our soldiers. We are there for them and their families, especially during the holidays. This is why I'm thankful for you today. You help us ensure that no soldier feels forgotten during Christmas -- whether through a card to uplift them, a warm meal to remind them of home or internet access so they can call their families. You help us make the difference for them. From all of us at the USO, thank you for the care you've shown our heroes. We wish you and your loved ones a very happy holiday season. For the People Who Serve, Michael S. Linnington Lieutenant General, U.S. Army, Retired Chief Executive Officer and President, USO United Service Organizations, Inc. name: Speaker Joanna McClinton
subject: My hope for you this New Year
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Joanna McClinton
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sjm2025email?refcode=header [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sjm2025email?refcode=header]
Happy New Year!
As we step into a new year, I’m filled with gratitude and hope for what lies ahead. A new year brings new opportunities. New opportunities to keep serving our communities, to keep building progress together, and to keep believing in what’s possible when we lead with purpose and compassion.
I am deeply thankful for your support and for the trust you place in me. It is a blessing to serve alongside you and for you.
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP06/1/83310/images/jm_newyear.png]
May this New Year bring you peace, good health, and joy in the days ahead. Here’s to a New Year full of purpose, possibility, and progress.
With Blessings Today and all the Days of 2026,
Rep. Joanna E. McClinton
Speaker of the Pennsylvania House
https://www.facebook.com/Joanna4PA [https://www.facebook.com/Joanna4PA] https://x.com/joanna4pa [https://x.com/joanna4pa] https://www.instagram.com/joanna_mcclinton/ [https://www.instagram.com/joanna_mcclinton/] https://bsky.app/profile/joanna4pa.bsky.social [https://bsky.app/profile/joanna4pa.bsky.social] https://www.tiktok.com/@joannamcclinton [https://www.tiktok.com/@joannamcclinton] https://www.mcclintonforpa.com/ [https://www.mcclintonforpa.com/]
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com.
Joanna McClinton serves as the Speaker of the Pennsylvania State House, while proudly serving the 191st district.
Joanna has made important strides to enact progress in the Pennsylvania Legislature. From working to protect the rights of those accused in our criminal justice system to spearheading the fight to expand our voting rights, she has proven that she will always prioritize the prosperity of Pennsylvanians over the promise of power. If you're committed to joining this fight, you can click here to make a one-time donation, [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sjm2025email?refcode=footer] or you can click here to make a monthly recurring donation. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sjm2025email?refcode=footer&recurring=1]

# **Merry Christmas** 🎄✨
As we celebrate the birth of our Savior, Laura and I want to wish you and your family a joyful, peaceful, and blessed Christmas.
> _“But the angel said to them, ‘Do not be afraid. I bring you good news that will cause great joy for all the people.
> Today in the town of David a Savior has been born to you; He is the Messiah, the Lord.’” — Luke 2:10–11_
We’re especially thankful for the opportunity to serve this incredible community, and for the continued support, encouragement, and prayers from so many of you. From our family to yours, may this season bring warmth, hope, and time with the people you love. Merry Christmas! — Juan & Laura Ciscomani

name: Generations for America Foundation
subject: Looking forward to2026?
body:
Generations for America Foundation Ready to Fly in 2026

| | |
---
**HAPPY**
---
**HOLIDAYS**
---
**D. P.!**
---
| | **From the Team at**
**Generations for America and Generations for America Foundation**

---
**Dear Friends and Supporters,** **As we celebrate this season of gratitude and reflection, we want to extend our heartfelt appreciation to every one of you who has supported Generations for America over the past three years. Your belief in our mission has fueled our progress, strengthened our reach, and empowered us to continue advocating for the constitutional principles that form the foundation of our great nation.** **This year, we have made tremendous strides through the _Ingrid Centurion Podcast_ , where we engage in critical discussions on constitutional battles and civic responsibility. Our _Shield of Rights Courses_ continue to grow, educating Americans of all ages on the importance of constitutional literacy, while our _Roberts Rules of Order_ training equips future leaders with the tools they need to conduct meetings with integrity, discipline, and respect for process. We added four new board members with expertise and extensive experience in law enforcement and public safety, event management, international cultural affairs and strategic planning. Together, we are building an informed and capable generation—one ready to protect, defend, and carry forward the torch of liberty.** **As the holidays approach, we wish you and your families a joyful and peaceful Christmas season, and a prosperous New Year. May your homes be filled with warmth, love, and blessings, and may the coming year bring growth, opportunity, and continued strength to our community.** **Thank you for standing with us. We look forward to achieving even greater milestones together in the year ahead.** **With gratitude and hope,** **Ingrid Centurion and the Boards of Generations for America and**
**Generations for America Foundation**
---
| |
---
|
# English First Classes
---
All our attendees want to improve their language skills, including improving their English and having conversations in English and Spanish. These classes are designed for native Spanish speakers to improve their English. We hold them at a local church and welcome anyone regardless of denomination or nationality.
---
| |
---
|
# Public Speaker Training
---
Public speaking can be daunting and challenging for the speaker to reach and motivate audiences. Generations for America Foundation can help you deliver more professional and eloquent talks that target your audience. We can help you.
---
| |
---
|
# Black History Month
---
Generations for America was instrumental in organizing the Gala event in 2023 and Ingrid delivered an inspiring speech!
---
| |
---
|
# Slam the Scam Public Safety
---
>From cyber threats to violence we must always be aware to protect our families and staff. Criminals often call, leave a voicemail, send a seemingly innocent social media connection, or email correspondence from something that looks legitimate. Don't be a victim. We've spoken at local libraries about the modern day dangers that you need to be aware of to protect yourself and you family.
---
| |
---
|
# Shield of Rights Constitutional Webinars
---
A program that ensures everyone knows their rights and responsibilities. Special focus on law enforcement perspective is crucial for both officers and the public. A well-rounded program can help ensure that everyone knows their rights and responsibilities, leading to better community relations and more informed decision-making in law enforcement. Local libraries were given one-hour highlights. Ingrid delivered a rousing speech about the Willful Subversion of the U.S. Constitution in Greenville.
---
| |
---
|
# North Charleston Mayoral Debate
---
Generations for America, a 501(C)(4), organized and moderated a public forum debate of six mayoral candidates at North Charleston City Hall. Our non-partisan efforts help the voters ask candidates the tough questions.
---
| |
---
|
# Penmanship Camp for Kids
---
Our Penmanship Camp is designed to help children improve their handwriting skills, focusing on both print and cursive writing. Through music, history and fun engaging activities, children will develop proper writing techniques, including letter formation, spacing, and fluidity.
---
| |
---
|
# Robert's Rules of Order
---
Develop effective participatory decision-making skills. Develop skills to be able to serve as a competent committee chairman or member. Develop skills to be able to function well as an organizational officer or member. Understand the rules and how they function. Register for our next course starting on January 7th at [https://www.generationsforamericafoundation.com/events](http://link.rby20.com/ls/click?upn=u001.GAk7fHL-2BVi5MccRb6usJku2BaI7VtpWwTdcB7N5mOlDZuFDPpLku3h2bIoC0Emk50FHUT-2FjCp-2BsJXwRtrT0bCg-3D-3D9B7C_9XnTPLnaEjN9OHWhKYUffDeECIVzR9M-2B5LaO5RH8NVCvXrI5zrvAmWKlzz2BTRM7s8INkI5SckbDmt-2BWdjKKGbLk6cfcI6HcTo-2FzwJlVunOo1mcou5HeXnw7GlMEdlJgd6g6j53PdD-2FHmW90eAtVu35Q1eoLuJcmahBUbL2-2FNjpRwRtOvl3mHM-2FwrvWzIiAqXoy8FJC8mj-2FtQg5ie6z2-2F6GMio-2B3CYvBj3BYpxnBLqRj4HIU6t-2F1ChRuCvdcPB0T9qpCxCBpoShOsP4gs-2BA13AlULbY6lIUNmYzeKRj4DQxK04bfSBYM7FZ7OklLGt03sF0L8J1H55GVwRJqMd8m7V4ZfL4vkCrtOlFqnvHnM9tZlEDuZnDAeCYG3Y7J2Ua4-2FDKP4B2Hm4-2F1x6YKgb7GDs5wylOuobXhjJ5u8lSHsut3-2FM3mWuoQc9PBhh2B5DTqlfsuGI6jvMk02IHY59xR69nvW9FhF-2BBySr0iekzDxdaSSXNG0PxMId0KV9EPnuECxeRPhjbf317yhHDBYtkIIQ-3D-3D).
---
| |
---
|
# Expungement Clinics
---
Ingrid Centurion explains the importance of getting your criminal record expunged on Fox24 News Now. See her interview at [https://youtu.be/TqJZdw2Fkws?si=WS6GjTb3AxQI8A75](http://link.rby20.com/ls/click?upn=u001.GAk7fHL-2BVi5MccRb6usJkt0aOAw1OCFJaSonkTXecf-2BMLG9OKHczpkqM-2Bm3nlFLkD4jOnzbhzRfPct4SoDzNJw-3D-3Ds03c_9XnTPLnaEjN9OHWhKYUffDeECIVzR9M-2B5LaO5RH8NVCvXrI5zrvAmWKlzz2BTRM7s8INkI5SckbDmt-2BWdjKKGbLk6cfcI6HcTo-2FzwJlVunOo1mcou5HeXnw7GlMEdlJgd6g6j53PdD-2FHmW90eAtVu35Q1eoLuJcmahBUbL2-2FNjpRwRtOvl3mHM-2FwrvWzIiAqXoy8FJC8mj-2FtQg5ie6z2-2F6GMio-2B3CYvBj3BYpxnBLqRj4HIU6t-2F1ChRuCvdcPB0T9qpCxCBpoShOsP4gs-2BA13AlULbY6lIUNmYzeKRj4DQxK04bfSBYM7FZ7OklLGt03sF0L8J1H55GVwRJqMd8m7V4ZfL4vkCrtOlFqnvHnM9tS6P52fjr4-2FdF-2BeTccJxrsJ2N45pP4yMu-2FyZvwTJNJoc3zV4o7UcaEGWudYsObUso4qkWOLn6fOSUlaIcT5qloHKkyhfOx6vxfXdBo8-2F9Iqt5giQhXOToE9LeAS8k5P669OfLTLFNw-2BaaHEpAhgTs0AKXjnjLJ7eE1gvhtskdWmg-3D-3D).
---
| |
---
|
# Name It, Change It, Do It
---
Generations for America Foundation published a motivational book called _Name It, Change It, Do It_ available for purchase on [LuLu](http://link.rby20.com/ls/click?upn=u001.GAk7fHL-2BVi5MccRb6usJkhLu-2F6uBdjzzExSeySaOKN6YO6CdtMA3HvKYlBg4s8CNxAk1ga9FxZsSA419UIirTYMxksfv841oX1GOC2FvFwz-2BCe-2BdXD-2BzT9wx4c1I-2Fu3R22PjZlKqFKWa6nTDVhJoWv4keOMZqxiZXkUdKMWlo8b36IobJXPdpUAPIqfirPCYzGC48rSGxVDyb30W0p3ajntyr9Ed011FBamQcxw38Ag-3DatZb_9XnTPLnaEjN9OHWhKYUffDeECIVzR9M-2B5LaO5RH8NVCvXrI5zrvAmWKlzz2BTRM7s8INkI5SckbDmt-2BWdjKKGbLk6cfcI6HcTo-2FzwJlVunOo1mcou5HeXnw7GlMEdlJgd6g6j53PdD-2FHmW90eAtVu35Q1eoLuJcmahBUbL2-2FNjpRwRtOvl3mHM-2FwrvWzIiAqXoy8FJC8mj-2FtQg5ie6z2-2F6GMio-2B3CYvBj3BYpxnBLqRj4HIU6t-2F1ChRuCvdcPB0T9qpCxCBpoShOsP4gs-2BA13AlULbY6lIUNmYzeKRj4DQxK04bfSBYM7FZ7OklLGt03sF0L8J1H55GVwRJqMd8m7V4ZfL4vkCrtOlFqnvHnM9t8tPwQqtY83lZa68dD086gtZ-2B1GufdqEp0Vc2gNVZs7IPaZBYpOFOEwczC9bWWYvrEHr4th7rm6JfI2iOWsAEOIrw9ZbhpQdZi1tiiKzw-2Bi8XFssPeOD0oO2VsLThRyuN-2FcTpXFhAyTn4CnLnpCPWu7JVuNn3WOK40fgdReQfCwQ-3D-3D). We followed that up with an empowerment event based on the practical wisdom from the book called Accelerate & Activate a Change Conference in North Charleston.
---
| |
---
|
# Political Candidate Meet and Greets
---
Generations for America, a 501(C)(4), organized and provided event night support for political candidates running for U.S. Senate, SC Governor and SC Attorney General. We encourage candidates to reach out to the voters and we will give non-partisan event support.
---
| |
---
|
# Ingrid Centurion Podcast
---
Ingrid and her guests discuss and debate timely public affairs topics concerning critical issues many of which involve the U.S. Constitution. Watch episodes now at the [Ingrid Centurion Podcast.](http://link.rby20.com/ls/click?upn=u001.GAk7fHL-2BVi5MccRb6usJksHk-2FAqNLAPh2qBboYXGfSCtc5BWOgp3koXaeAqw5qcATSllRMGbq1jeoLrvvPA-2Bgg-3D-3D-t2V_9XnTPLnaEjN9OHWhKYUffDeECIVzR9M-2B5LaO5RH8NVCvXrI5zrvAmWKlzz2BTRM7s8INkI5SckbDmt-2BWdjKKGbLk6cfcI6HcTo-2FzwJlVunOo1mcou5HeXnw7GlMEdlJgd6g6j53PdD-2FHmW90eAtVu35Q1eoLuJcmahBUbL2-2FNjpRwRtOvl3mHM-2FwrvWzIiAqXoy8FJC8mj-2FtQg5ie6z2-2F6GMio-2B3CYvBj3BYpxnBLqRj4HIU6t-2F1ChRuCvdcPB0T9qpCxCBpoShOsP4gs-2BA13AlULbY6lIUNmYzeKRj4DQxK04bfSBYM7FZ7OklLGt03sF0L8J1H55GVwRJqMd8m7V4ZfL4vkCrtOlFqnvHnM9u9Y-2Bu1FG6pgvqK2CJU53nVsPTcs3ZhptGZWZxNIYLsdLjDHSCiJiFVzNfRoAunt8xRKcrrNu-2FV8HZ50vLXXyFhrEvRnV1gwF0zrlN1VdjQKA-2FNSoiApaAxe6qi0DnGj8a1AnPP-2F2lq0I-2B6lsnTYxXXUURo56FCqHDrur-2BdaU4Drg-3D-3D) Please contact us to become a sponsor of the show or an episode. We're also looking for guests who are authorities in their field or public figures who wish to talk about topics on the show.

clean_body: Generations for America Foundation Ready to Fly in 2026 | | | HAPPY HOLIDAYS D. P.! | | From the Team at Generations for America and Generations for America Foundation Dear Friends and Supporters, As we celebrate this season of gratitude and reflection, we want to extend our heartfelt appreciation to every one of you who has supported Generations for America over the past three years. Your belief in our mission has fueled our progress, strengthened our reach, and empowered us to continue advocating for the constitutional principles that form the foundation of our great nation. This year, we have made tremendous strides through the Ingrid Centurion Podcast , where we engage in critical discussions on constitutional battles and civic responsibility. Our Shield of Rights Courses continue to grow, educating Americans of all ages on the importance of constitutional literacy, while our Roberts Rules of Order training equips future leaders with the tools they need to conduct meetings with integrity, discipline, and respect for process. We added four new board members with expertise and extensive experience in law enforcement and public safety, event management, international cultural affairs and strategic planning. Together, we are building an informed and capable generation—one ready to protect, defend, and carry forward the torch of liberty. As the holidays approach, we wish you and your families a joyful and peaceful Christmas season, and a prosperous New Year. May your homes be filled with warmth, love, and blessings, and may the coming year bring growth, opportunity, and continued strength to our community. Thank you for standing with us. We look forward to achieving even greater milestones together in the year ahead. With gratitude and hope, Ingrid Centurion and the Boards of Generations for America and Generations for America Foundation | | | English First Classes All our attendees want to improve their language skills, including improving their English and having conversations in English and Spanish. These classes are designed for native Spanish speakers to improve their English. We hold them at a local church and welcome anyone regardless of denomination or nationality. | | | Public Speaker Training Public speaking can be daunting and challenging for the speaker to reach and motivate audiences. Generations for America Foundation can help you deliver more professional and eloquent talks that target your audience. We can help you. | | | Black History Month Generations for America was instrumental in organizing the Gala event in 2023 and Ingrid delivered an inspiring speech! | | | Slam the Scam Public Safety From cyber threats to violence we must always be aware to protect our families and staff. Criminals often call, leave a voicemail, send a seemingly innocent social media connection, or email correspondence from something that looks legitimate. Don't be a victim. We've spoken at local libraries about the modern day dangers that you need to be aware of to protect yourself and you family. | | | Shield of Rights Constitutional Webinars A program that ensures everyone knows their rights and responsibilities. Special focus on law enforcement perspective is crucial for both officers and the public. A well-rounded program can help ensure that everyone knows their rights and responsibilities, leading to better community relations and more informed decision-making in law enforcement. Local libraries were given one-hour highlights. Ingrid delivered a rousing speech about the Willful Subversion of the U.S. Constitution in Greenville. | | | North Charleston Mayoral Debate Generations for America, a 501(C)(4), organized and moderated a public forum debate of six mayoral candidates at North Charleston City Hall. Our non-partisan efforts help the voters ask candidates the tough questions. | | | Penmanship Camp for Kids Our Penmanship Camp is designed to help children improve their handwriting skills, focusing on both print and cursive writing. Through music, history and fun engaging activities, children will develop proper writing techniques, including letter formation, spacing, and fluidity. | | | Robert's Rules of Order Develop effective participatory decision-making skills. Develop skills to be able to serve as a competent committee chairman or member. Develop skills to be able to function well as an organizational officer or member. Understand the rules and how they function. Register for our next course starting on January 7th at . | | | Expungement Clinics Ingrid Centurion explains the importance of getting your criminal record expunged on Fox24 News Now. See her interview at . | | | Name It, Change It, Do It Generations for America Foundation published a motivational book called Name It, Change It, Do It available for purchase on . We followed that up with an empowerment event based on the practical wisdom from the book called Accelerate & Activate a Change Conference in North Charleston. | | | Political Candidate Meet and Greets Generations for America, a 501(C)(4), organized and provided event night support for political candidates running for U.S. Senate, SC Governor and SC Attorney General. We encourage candidates to reach out to the voters and we will give non-partisan event support. | | | Ingrid Centurion Podcast Ingrid and her guests discuss and debate timely public affairs topics concerning critical issues many of which involve the U.S. Constitution. Watch episodes now at the Please contact us to become a sponsor of the show or an episode. We're also looking for guests who are authorities in their field or public figures who wish to talk about topics on the show. | | | | Please support our continued efforts in 2026 to inform, educate and inspire Americans both natural born and those that now call the USA their new home. We're glad you cherish our freedom and opportunity and want to help you succeed. Learn details about . Membership is available to participate in our educational offerings. name: Brett Lindstrom
subject: Merry Christmas from Nebraska
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Brett Lindstrom for Congress
body:
from my family to yours!
| | | | [](https://link.win.lindstromforne.)
---
| | Peter,
Today, across Nebraska, families are gathering around the table, attending church, and spending time with the people they love. I wanted to take a moment to wish you a Merry Christmas.

---
[](https://link.win.lindstromforne.)
Christmas is a time to reflect on what matters most — faith, family, and the values that define our way of life here in Nebraska. It’s a reminder of the importance of hard work, community, and looking out for one another.
I’m grateful for Nebraska families who serve their neighbors, support their communities, and pass on strong values to the next generation. Your commitment to faith and family is what makes our state such a special place to call home.
No matter where today finds you, I hope you’re able to slow down, enjoy time with loved ones, and reflect on the blessings we’ve been given.
From my family to yours, I wish you peace, joy, and a very Merry Christmas.
God bless you, and God bless Nebraska.
Thank you,
Brett Lindstrom

| | |
---
| | **Stay Connected with Team Lindstrom**

President Trump’s tariffs will : > >>IGNITE an economic BOOM > >>SLASH the national debt > >>PUT money back in the American people’s pockets. Don’t wait. For a stronger America,Dr. Janak JoshiRepublican for U.S. Senate PAID AND AUTHORIZED BY JANAK JOSHI FOR SENATE PO Box 21174, Roanoke, VA, 24018 |

name: Haley Stevens
subject: Happy holidays!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Haley Stevens for Senate
body:
I wanted to send a quick note to say thank you

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. [To unsubscribe from this email list, please click here.](https://act.haleyformi.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?&t=1&akid=1734%2E49239%2EPDj1IO)
| | |
---
[](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/10849?t=2&akid=1734%2E49239%2EPDj1IO)
Happy holidays, null!

I hope today brings you a moment of peace, a delicious meal, and time with the people you love.
Before I unplug to celebrate Christmas with my family, I wanted to send a quick note to simply say _thank you_.
This campaign has been one of the most meaningful journeys of my life. I love Michigan. I love fighting for Michigan. And I know none of it would be possible without this grassroots team standing strong beside me.
From union halls to diners to factory floors, I’ve met so many incredible Michiganders this year. You’ve encouraged me. You’ve shared your stories – stories of struggle, resilience, and hope – and you remind me every single day why I’m in this fight.

So wherever you are and however you’re celebrating, know how grateful I am to be in this with you.__ We’ve got a big year ahead. But today, I hope you get to rest, reflect, and enjoy being with the people who matter most to you. Wishing you a joyful and peaceful holiday season.
With grit, gratitude, and holiday cheer,
Haley
|
* * *
**A note from Haley Stevens:** null, I’m proud to be a lifelong Michigander and the daughter of small-business owners. That’s why I’ve fought so hard for working families my entire life. Now, I’m running for Michigan’s open Senate seat to continue fighting for the people and communities I love. The path to the Senate majority runs right through Michigan, which means this race is shaping up to be the most competitive and expensive in the entire country. The GOP is already coming after me as a top target for defeat, so I’m counting on your grassroots support to fight back and win. [**If you’re in this fight with me, will you add your name to endorse my campaign?**](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/10850?t=3&akid=1734%2E49239%2EPDj1IO) | | | [](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/10849?t=4&akid=1734%2E49239%2EPDj1IO)
---
You can also text “JOIN” to 80242 to become a core member of our team and receive exclusive, real-time updates from my campaign.
Thank you,
Haley
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com.
[To unsubscribe from this email list, please click here.](https://act.haleyformi.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?&t=5&akid=1734%2E49239%2EPDj1IO)
Paid for by Haley Stevens for Senate.
33717 Woodward Ave #539
Birmingham, MI 48009

name: James Talarico
subject: A movement that loves our neighbors
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Talarico for Texas
body:
#
Happy holidays, Peter!
I wanted to update you on some of the work that our volunteers have been doing to serve our neighbors.
In November, our volunteers organized food bank volunteer shifts in 11 cities across the state to support communities navigating Republicans’ SNAP cuts during the recent government shutdown.
And this month, we continued showing up and loving our neighbors by helping to organize toy drives across Texas — collecting over 2,000 toys for children in need as we head into the holidays. Take a look:
**This campaign is about more than politics. We are building a movement dedicated to loving our neighbors — not just in word, but in action.**
Yes, we’re doing phone banks. Yes, we’re starting block walks. Yes, we’re doing the things volunteers traditionally do on a political campaign.
But we’re also doing more than that — showing up for our neighbors who need us the most. With more than 10,000 volunteers, there’s no limit to what we can do for our communities.
We are showing what it looks like to lead with love and meet people where they are.
With love and blessings to you and yours,
James Talarico
p.s. If you want to join the Team Talarico fun, there’s plenty more coming. **[Join our volunteer team now to learn what’s ahead!](https://act.jamestalarico.com/signup/volunteer_2026?source=em251225&t=1&akid=342%2E1347274%2E57ULpq)**

Paid for by Talarico for Texas
Talarico for Texas
PO Box 14508
Austin, TX 78761
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. If you need to update your contact information, [**you can submit this form.**](https://act.jamestalarico.com/signup/update-info/?t=2&akid=342%2E1347274%2E57ULpq) Looking for Team Talarico merch? [**Check out our online store!**](https://store.jamestalarico.com?utm_source=em_footer&utm_campaign=em_footer) This is a grassroots movement to take back Texas for working people. [**Please consider making a contribution to support our fight today.**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jt-em-launch?refcode=em_footer&t=3&refcode2=342_1347274_57ULpq&akid=342%2E1347274%2E57ULpq)
James is grateful to have you in our movement. We try to send only the most important updates and opportunities to participate via email, but if you want to, you can [unsubscribe here](https://act.jamestalarico.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe?t=4&akid=342%2E1347274%2E57ULpq).

| | | For to us a child is born, to us a son is given, and the government will be on his shoulders. And he will be called Wonderful Counselor, Mighty God, Everlasting Father, Prince of Peace. Isaiah 9:6
Peter, as 2025 draws to a close, I want to thank you for all your work and contributions to the campaign.

One of the first scriptures I ever learned was John 3:16: For God so loved the world that He gave His only begotten Son, that whosoever believeth in Him should not perish, but have everlasting life. That is what Christmas is all about to me. God loves you! May God send you peace and joy! Have a wonderful Christmas.

With your help, the Golden Age of 2026 is coming.

name: Seth Moulton
subject: Wishing you a peaceful Christmas
body:
| |
---
Friend, On Christmas morning, before the day fills up, I always try to pause and remember what this day is really about. For our family, that means a little chaos, a lot of laughter, and two very excited kids reminding us how much the simple moments matter. Liz and I are grateful to be home in Massachusetts, grounded in the community and traditions that make this place feel like home. [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121956859/591129413/-1369401587?refcode=251225_sm_xmas&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjUvMS83NDc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxMTk2M2E1Ni1hNmUxLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=2V2CyJH2j925DwfUWs6tnZc1rdzwUPzEzGnnqWqkeIc=&emci=6b55c2bd-81df-f011-8194-000d3a1ec8c6&emdi=11963a56-a6e1-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ceid=194769) Christmas is also a time to reflect on service. I’ve seen, both in uniform and in public office, how much our country depends on people who quietly show up and take responsibility for one another. That sense of duty is something Massachusetts has always understood, and it’s something our country needs more of right now. I know this has been a heavy year for many families. But days like today are a reminder of why leadership matters and why public service should always be about people, not power. From my family to yours, I hope today brings you peace, warmth, and time with the people you love. Merry Christmas. — Seth
| | |
---|---|---

### About Seth Moulton
Seth Moulton is a father, a husband, a Marine, and a Democrat with a mission to bring a new generation of leaders to Congress. He is the proud father of two girls, Emmy and Caroline, and husband to his wife, Liz. They live in Salem, Massachusetts.
---

clean_body: | | Friend, On Christmas morning, before the day fills up, I always try to pause and remember what this day is really about. For our family, that means a little chaos, a lot of laughter, and two very excited kids reminding us how much the simple moments matter. Liz and I are grateful to be home in Massachusetts, grounded in the community and traditions that make this place feel like home. Christmas is also a time to reflect on service. I’ve seen, both in uniform and in public office, how much our country depends on people who quietly show up and take responsibility for one another. That sense of duty is something Massachusetts has always understood, and it’s something our country needs more of right now. I know this has been a heavy year for many families. But days like today are a reminder of why leadership matters and why public service should always be about people, not power. From my family to yours, I hope today brings you peace, warmth, and time with the people you love. Merry Christmas. — Seth | | | ---|---|--- About Seth Moulton Seth Moulton is a father, a husband, a Marine, and a Democrat with a mission to bring a new generation of leaders to Congress. He is the proud father of two girls, Emmy and Caroline, and husband to his wife, Liz. They live in Salem, Massachusetts. Follow Seth | | | | ---|---|---|--- Paid for by Seth for Massachusetts Email is a critical way to stay in touch with our supporters, but if you wish to unsubscribe, you may do so . Seth for Massachusetts PO Box 2013 Salem, MA 01970 United States

name: Lt. Col. David Flippo
subject: Merry Christmas, Nevada!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by David Flippo for Nevada]( "Paid for by David Flippo for Nevada")
body:
I want to wish you and your family a very Merry Christmas! |
---
| | [ ](https://electdavidflippo.us14.list-manage.com/track/click?u=9aba26b775663b53df718fc1a&id=5a3c947fcf&e=273c9a71fb)
---
| | Peter ,

As we celebrate Christmas, I want to take a moment to wish you and your loved ones a very Merry Christmas.

This season invites us to pause from the busyness of everyday life and reflect on what truly matters. For many families, Christmas is centered on faith and the celebration of Jesus Christ's birth, a reminder of God’s love, grace, and the hope that light brings into a sometimes dark world.
---
|
---
| “And she shall bring forth a son, and thou shalt call his name JESUS: for he shall save his people from their sins.”
\- Matthew 1:21 KJV
Christmas is also a time of gratitude. I am thankful for the strong families, churches, and communities across Nevada’s 4th District that embody generosity and compassion throughout the year, and especially during this season. Whether through volunteering, caring for neighbors, or simply checking in on one another, these quiet acts of kindness define the best of who we are.
From my family to yours, I wish you a Christmas filled with peace, warmth, and joyful moments shared with those you hold dear. May the message of Christmas bring comfort, renewed faith, and optimism for the year ahead.
Merry Christmas, and may God bless you, your family, Nevada, and the United States of America.
---
| For Nevada, David Flippo
Republican Candidate for U.S. Congress
Nevada’s 4th Congressional District
**[www.Flippo4NV.com](https://electdavidflippo.us14.list-manage.com/track/click?u=9aba26b775663b53df718fc1a&id=998f2d0b35&e=273c9a71fb)**

body:
Peter,

**[BIG NEWS:](https://links.iterable.com/s/c/l3e83YJnzb6jIbmDOCZgYcLAXDUoOagh3CSS824m-FmAEeQwvU0Guii3H3-Q0x7jI4xuUZbponwAQDpqCkXT8Mz0Gu-JGE3rJR3jk7V7UpJRdQxgN8YJtwkVyUWwjaDMzF5DikuWeAF15Quf4HigdJTbh6qljYXfSOkJXoCpLDe76IjvbinRsk5zGxmzqDd06Zolqk2YTjT76GLVe99x1tFsZBqyZRw6nDoA0lLMAY8DoW2tJdr6pGAxm3VPRGZzoA9wvzedFnVO8bBiMEhsEPeEwdJvClwHUXLCBRaz_85QH6WJ9cu_GFR5XyJgNpPEbcLYz4KB6XknxCCPQUCcrqydb_Lnvwx2wopqkk4EKI6tdJyEt2f5iuu2RRvhbhgWsfWlD5MWHxUJOFuq3F2h9UmalznoQPJWAAbFkdZ_xybZIf-0P_tfsIC776900FrhV7iM6mmhrL_xph2A58GGg_N_TXFPao0IALHNmrE5w811U8X93ZhYQFZx3RR0_tXpooOvgiILJ7guG0oDBLTzzIEEBV6A_ifsxBoE5yIEettF8rp4Vqw-kbo5CxWEPHoSPghF6WsbPcWqZ7cO-Vqm02_Qek86ZQSmsf3DXBbIepFejF6vqVrcTdMO89mwP89_6A6shrf478ROrGtGNkcSktk00kgBdpMQqO-zbePRTf9vn_qdRA2026zysOXXIhgbRZNwc1GorMLFB2B7ImSNlxPcs4slTcoosbdYTTdao7AReb2rBmRiTW7c-PgJirvGdQqcepKQg3_pwRV4w_qye30k041u7J5r_9ZePD0yp1bNRbAJ0S3awd41OLNODsGkR9oNIh13OFjyaaDs2K5aVvGeb4SSV6VXEVTxxBxMRzpRRfAblqiaWm9hj3KEhrKSoYoPXOenrAqtiIOTw6cuSlSp84TyYQb_CVJ-onwnsUkzUDuTlmuRET8TVKi0GU5VmQ5txqbEiuEUfnQicKr805YTHqxb2v36m_A7IR_Xc07sQv8WLNyY4cUpGSE7I_2ufN9KGFvacf5zt6aPVjyAiOQm0VewauE3VaTLqw-Ckl1Ff7THYkAE/_F_QHWM-nqgLU8cVNYbasW8eg444TZcC/16) **Republicans now have an 8-POINT LEAD over Democrats for control of the House and Senate.

But here's the thing...

_... None of that matters unless EVERY Republican completes and verifies their Voter Profile for 2025._

| | Today is a time to celebrate faith, family, and the blessings we share as Americans. I am truly grateful for the privilege of serving you in Congress and for the hardworking families who make the Hudson Valley such a special place to call home.
As you gather with loved ones, I pray this Christmas brings you joy, rest, and renewed hope for the year ahead.
Thank you for your continued support.
Sincerely,
Congressman Mike Lawler

name: From President Trump
subject: To: A Very Loyal MAGA Patriot
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Trump National Committee JFC, Inc
body:
Merry Christmas!
| | | | [](https://link.win.donaldjtrump.)
---
Dear Friend,
Today, I want to wish you and your family a very Merry Christmas.
This holy season is a time to reflect on faith, family, and the values that made America the greatest nation on Earth.
I truly believe God spared my life for a reason on that sickening day in Butler, PA. America was founded on Christian principles, and I am grateful that He has given me the purpose of fighting every day to Save America.
**Your support has meant more to me than I can say. Because of patriots like you, we are still standing strong.**
As a small token of my appreciation, I have a special Christmas surprise gift I would like to send directly to my supporters.[ **Please, confirm your address here: >**](https://link.win.donaldjtrump.)
Please consider this my way of saying thank you, Merry Christmas, and God bless you for standing with me when our country needs it the most.
[CONFIRM ADDRESS](https://link.win.donaldjtrump.)
**[I would deeply appreciate it if you chipped in any amount: it can be SMALL or very GENEROUS before my end-of-year fundraising deadline.>](https://link.win.donaldjtrump.)**
With faith in God and love for this great country, we will continue the fight to protect the America we cherish.
**God bless you and your family,**
**And may God bless the United States of America.**
Sincerely,
Donald J. Trump

[](https://link.win.donaldjtrump.)

name: Bryce Reeves
subject: Where My Commitment to Virginia Began
body:
My story starts on the front lines – not in politics.

| | | | [ ](https://links.email.brycereeves.com/els/v4/iguiiaine9v4b4hdxdtuv/Yy3HiX6lUEDmvhuW3nb2D2dnExst2EzIWmuF2Ta-5634_Gp3lx9amzBA_r7c1Pz2YeNw/3g_Oirg3VDoWF0bBAMFF1zPEmSV4eS1ZdHPoA40huHOEuoqb_zFesrvmwuRLy6I02JYKcBXxIjcKcH0Yrgx_JWyvqHB12h8BzZ09XtTN3pbkmSMUA7-4_MSfpavds-X4z1HnPv8rOrCDlX-dCyLApV0-B9f2xrG9hu34xqgHQErnieNvb_F23BL7WOxm68QO1FHOGD0fCYJ7aB7PQGP1vFup7t54sAW7ZfawvHwrwaVQVUMXycRVWnDyjqhWeQcTv3aHzKjGtv7kOAf4ZdosVXPaWalhOrbAqfDwvBotrxq5CXfbf0proEts4d5T16HsbdnutoNvgUbV8SECISpkvZO3xxEoN6ORjZHyQYnUJhDRA0FCnRoICsjcuBIU)
---
| | Friend, long before I ever ran for public office, I learned what service truly means. **I didn’t learn it from politics – I learned it from a life on the front lines.** In the Army, you learn quickly that leadership isn’t something you claim. It’s something you prove – _**through discipline, sacrifice, and the responsibility to protect the people standing beside you.**_ In law enforcement, you see the courage of the men and women who put their lives on the line every day for their communities – **_and you understand what real accountability looks like._** Those experiences shaped me far more than anything in Richmond or Washington ever could. **And they’re the reason I’m running for the United States Senate.**
---
| | | [ SUPPORT REEVES ](https://links.email.brycereeves.com/els/v4/b_yoelxlmrdb71yixcyxh/Yy3HiX6lUEDmvhuW3nb2D2dnExst2EzIWmuF2Ta-5634_Gp3lx9amzBA_r7c1Pz2YeNw/3g_Oirg3VDoWF0bBAMFF1zPEmSV4eS1ZdHPoA40huKOzH4zYI-GOlhC6AypG51MeOZD3HAYAGA3EoFkIm5KdOIk9cMTl26puAvbnLBbnMu-jFg7SSKE0UeSUpAmvZcuE4GyQBZG0vfV0awHAAN1ONEfBJIuctpbs1E_GgxUWKKpT4ef6i62y0MUP1bectcuakiSdH-Q4asrJh1WKF7dVJYjibhYC8hiNlbGoT-oeYUgIVF0lPXVozIlkOmpz8HobzXoI7M6DPiq46OFiuV0x6CW1KtlF2p6HBL2YibElgCuVCgtH-F6y97yLh_YwCh3WsiyKvofE6zi0jaVmH4Qqefq8GyYjTM8Rs1E-w53AqAIuVo3wwPTc_0RNJmEP)
---
| | Because right now, our country is being held back by politicians who have never been tested – who talk endlessly about leadership but rarely demonstrate it. Mark Warner has spent decades in Washington mastering the art of political survival. But Virginians deserve something better: **_a Senator who knows what it means to serve, to protect, and to put others first._** That’s the standard I live by. And it’s the standard I’ll bring to the Senate every single day.
---
| | | [ STAND WITH REEVES ](https://links.email.brycereeves.com/els/v4/chhtszefrazbyjecccbsd/Yy3HiX6lUEDmvhuW3nb2D2dnExst2EzIWmuF2Ta-5634_Gp3lx9amzBA_r7c1Pz2YeNw/3g_Oirg3VDoWF0bBAMFF1zPEmSV4eS1ZdHPoA40huMuclfldDkiJTx_6MAGNG02WNu07aksrj9tn60yZ_fWHnNFnHmwWbjhRsrZQIn2Q7t-NCwxABJE2EvUP8Y0VBtOF6ZZul9rEOkADmg7dfUWYMfgBMnCKEnf1gUzlXFfqkznkbGO_kxc6Q4sJr0NcnlgF1AEFDbqnDbafyPUkZiQpJ8uxTtGcH5uivg1mgPGP5WFwgpz8-0e1d4-yimkH9BbjthdioURa2W7pY6jI5gS3hhQp7wXSMxH6T4BRLPvF1KrB4_pKT2N93Us4UW1AgiqBHMUCKSy1A_748bD6NhBfg7DeltnI0OmK0KYLAdxkjAwocGO67nUDfHlSteQG)
---
| | This campaign is about restoring leadership that comes from experience, not from political calculation. [**_If you believe Virginia deserves a Senator shaped by service – not by Washington – I’m asking you to join me today. >>>_**](https://links.email.brycereeves.com/els/v4/kl7rupf8vnnshdzexurcf/Yy3HiX6lUEDmvhuW3nb2D2dnExst2EzIWmuF2Ta-5634_Gp3lx9amzBA_r7c1Pz2YeNw/3g_Oirg3VDoWF0bBAMFF1zPEmSV4eS1ZdHPoA40huPPzn4ck3yCRY2PJLc7z7UszcWXZpHCUrgWDi8H4SLwZeJM84wPFbNmZS8Tgn5O3o1Eem_b2NTqVEcKrFn1uIqsf1yGKSJ0JPLyT0RQm2gv6lW9Aw95amO7qr2XULj9QohV9SkwivIrT66nx-PF7NfgfTMVAhic4H-ljz1WIFFHRaW4f7twyZWCinfsHimvCxyjud9jJHNFaAzeP-qRLVyB8zvhkbNUz2MBr-4DKqxbGMTYcb6ZP10YgBvhFOsc1nGQTOmcqyJUogvboImwvLuCz0bVTiX820O5V9c0K1O7X5Vap89EJh_YjJf1iJ0ziQl2VIOLGF-qMKYnsLXDx)
---
| | | [ DONATE NOW - SUPPORT REEVES FOR SENATE ](https://links.email.brycereeves.com/els/v4/9yfdypotklpga3w1k3nlg/Yy3HiX6lUEDmvhuW3nb2D2dnExst2EzIWmuF2Ta-5634_Gp3lx9amzBA_r7c1Pz2YeNw/3g_Oirg3VDoWF0bBAMFF1zPEmSV4eS1ZdHPoA40huISimC9UkmaJhhtcM-TEpRmhCAQfcpTdNuINBYcmFf4pp9I1HUZoVCGM4gnVJHBKHDWMf3W3OqUNRo0LIItT2e-PQ1o9xV-Ylx71JvLULPCPm7uq0VOjb-4SHFk9b-rniLcsMYSgy5IJXzS8kYgfYrjlamYC3JqgO9m_oTuxjpipclmt61ERQyeqtJu2bUqcx8cwgvpvgSRIX6P8MDBumGWn3fJdU7YZ53fpB0i6E4iBWCAFLJ0crBOOYo8FgtVuulgM_vLfBL8IaMmspCmNNqTY-IbUruF0LvihMdbFSSNKfUoAZ3szxm8gBHZDXqxCfZsCd7mgunuA7KRGdsUx)

| | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.
