In a quiet town, we went in with a small crew and left with footage that looks nothing like a typical construction project.

No big signs, no press, no clear explanation—just a strange, humming build set off by itself on the edge of town.

The hardware, the security, the scale... absolutely shocking for a place this quiet.

This isn’t rumor or theory—it’s a live site, and what’s happening there could have serious implications for technology, business, and potentially for the markets over time.

You were never directly invited to look at this, which is why seeing it now—before it’s wrapped in headlines and commentary—matters.

[Watch this Virginia video while you still can and decide for yourself what this footage might mean for the future.](https://links.main.yourcapitalminds.com/u/click?_t=affa6bfca09a497f86454ba803e6a9a2&_m=543b0ccf408743b587d89b7c48733535&_e=-BVUlrwqFJsW8K1wYNg98jd5eAxQzqKhoc55JOMUYMlHnjpts2hb8iVC192B4r6VUcUmG1aCcbFuyEI1C8-rPsj1JBNDYn59ckMzPzNq6qOqibEOsQnkGgvCl30lUUN90eRyhZmNLvxIzXlzqGnwOQmw-OKGxLh3pZc8inTYcIZSp1VZ16F-BIs1HpF7qmbq_m0s4uZDx_fk6UZSo3QB_wubFEndQSJUP4_amnsy2_Ch69DjU93pKoB14WDH3gPogNSeNvC9pTN3vzaRCrUDNeq2D26tPMLjirc1K-JCLYDzWAHahEJnP0EEDFLscA33novjm0Dpob-5yT3vo0Eh3_w5bHXlJTuQDbWGm-fiNkM2xTFZTh6a-UUdCxsOFjPo8hlQwBErGQg0u5eYFLZwx4FyuJj3PHY4WvCzS7Xb-cPHWsTvB1l9LjCPbUvc8rGW4MaijvQZ4hHRpA1qcYOklCLD-Nh3coPeGswOxE4bqH3WwhrEn-O4nTZ4ord0KnMvnYTsSfukiuxD13wpPim2Oo_zkbGH8P7_gLxb5QPhnF6b5qVt-cjxmdOTks3HnKub54eUZbX2fdxIW1909lO42w%3D%3D)
Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671.

The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671. | | You are receiving this newsletter from Your Capital Minds (YCM), an esteemed member of the Stark Media LLC family. body:

| | | **Postal workers are the _HEROES_ of our holiday season!**

**[Sign the card to thank our nation's postal workers for working tirelessly to deliver _millions_ of gifts and holiday cards this season >>](http://go.dscc.org/go/22299?ask=5&t=6&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5%2C10%2C20%2C50&code=DPE-ND-20251225-SignTheCard-ThankPostalWorkers25RF-Pros-sl1&refcode=DPE-ND-20251225-SignTheCard-ThankPostalWorkers25RF-Pros-sl1&akid=22127%2E9716520%2Etust0p)**

Team, come snow, rain, heat, or gloom of night, the USPS has tirelessly ensured that Americans receive the mail they need. From delivering prescription drugs to Social Security checks to holiday gifts, our postal workers _always_ get the job done.

That's why this holiday season, we want to show our gratitude with a MASSIVE thank you card for our dedicated public servants.**We need 250,000 signatures before we can send it, and we're counting on you to help us get it to the finish line!**

**From:[ dpwillis67@gmail.com](http://go.dscc.org/go/22299?ask=5&t=8&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5%2C10%2C20%2C50&code=DPE-ND-20251225-SignTheCard-ThankPostalWorkers25RF-Pros-sl1&refcode=DPE-ND-20251225-SignTheCard-ThankPostalWorkers25RF-Pros-sl1&akid=22127%2E9716520%2Etust0p)**

name: Jenny Beth Martin, Tea Party Patriots Action
subject: Wishing you a Merry Christmas!
body:
A special Christmas message, episodes, and more!
[](https://emails.teapartypatriots.org/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwww.teapartypatriots.org/1/0100019b566dbd87-40690781-8b54-43b2-85a6-cb820eb986c2-000000/hHq3x8D4SWfSw2qDhReGpvHv_xqIqsM7Dq9mzcz_Up8=437)Patriot–Merry Christmas!Today we celebrate the miracle that changed the world and our hearts forever, the day Jesus Christ our Savior was born, and the beginning of God's gift of salvation to man. As you gather with your families and friends, may your home be filled with the peace and joy that only He can give. If you are celebrating alone this year, know that I am thinking of you and praying that you feel the Lord’s presence, because with Him, you are never alone.In the midst of everything we face as a nation, Christmas reminds us that God is sovereign, that truth endures, and that the light of Christ always overcomes darkness. That eternal hope is what strengthens us for every challenge we encounter in our lives.This year, [_**I put together a special Christmas card**_](https://emails.teapartypatriots.org/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwww.teapartypatriots.org%2Fchristmascard%2F/1/0100019b566dbd87-40690781-8b54-43b2-85a6-cb820eb986c2-000000/hHpUxKNof_vG6QllvOZaqJPerEkII6RxN9Q943XPbF0=437) for you and your family. It includes a short video message from me, along with our Advent series—brief reflections on the heart of Christmas. [](https://emails.teapartypatriots.org/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwww.teapartypatriots.org%2Fchristmascard%2F/2/0100019b566dbd87-40690781-8b54-43b2-85a6-cb820eb986c2-000000/vbNHXOuCJSH2ynD2K0B2eKZarq5GK6vRL1UryF7HAWw=437)We hope these videos will bless and encourage you this Christmas season. Please take a moment to watch and share them.From all of us at Tea Party Patriots Action, may God bless you and your family this Christmas and in the year ahead.Merry Christmas!Jenny Beth Martin and the entire Tea Party Patriots Action Team _This email list is a joint venture of the following separate organizations: Tea Party Patriots Action, Inc., Tea Party Patriots Foundation, and Tea Party Patriots Citizens Fund. All rights reserved.__Tea Party Patriots Action, Inc. operates as a social welfare organization under section 501(c)(4) of the Internal Revenue Code. Contributions to Tea Party Patriots Action, Inc. are not deductible as charitable contributions for income tax purposes._ To donate by mail, send your check made payable to:[_**Tea Party Patriots Action**_](https://emails.teapartypatriots.org/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwww.teapartypatriots.org%2Fdonate%2F/1/0100019b566dbd87-40690781-8b54-43b2-85a6-cb820eb986c2-000000/WbAil0z3gw7KB1dNk-lMmP4rTgHpgi2UpysKXmF-Zko=437)
2295 Towne Lake Pkwy Ste 116-314
Woodstock, GA 30189Want to change how you receive these emails?
You can [unsubscribe](https://emails.teapartypatriots.org/CL0/https:%2F%2Ftpot.teapartypatriots.org%2Fcontact-preferences%2F01jp3rk1m05jb3jy5xyw9vqr5t%3Fct=1ebd6a5833cd%26et=a8fe34ad85d9/1/0100019b566dbd87-40690781-8b54-43b2-85a6-cb820eb986c2-000000/CaIHCoQnHpaXtiokxCL-CwALnVA0RwFmJtbWiX4mQSo=437) or [update your preferences](https://emails.teapartypatriots.org/CL0/https:%2F%2Ftpot.teapartypatriots.org%2Fcontact-preferences%2F01jp3rk1m05jb3jy5xyw9vqr5t%3Fct=1ebd6a5833cd%26et=a8fe34ad85d9/2/0100019b566dbd87-40690781-8b54-43b2-85a6-cb820eb986c2-000000/nzbbm0dFt8KhV7KcO4KgPWx7e9AuuhuF0I1cAWHoJ4U=437).

**High school dropout. Waffle House waitress. First woman to graduate from The Citadel. Single mom. Fighter.** **Nancy Mace wasn’t born into power. She worked for every inch of ground she’s gained.** At 17, she dropped out of high school and took a job slinging hash browns at Waffle House to survive. No silver spoon. No Ivy League connections. Just grit, grace, and a whole lot of graveyard shifts. **By the grace of God, she turned that struggle into a story no politician can touch.** She made history as the first woman to graduate from The Citadel, South Carolina’s legendary military college. She built her own business. She raised two kids on her own. And when the Left came for everything she believed in, she stood her ground.Now, she’s running for Governor of South Carolina. Nancy Mace is a battle-tested conservative who’s walked through fire and come out swinging. In Congress, she’s been one of President Trump’s fiercest allies, fighting to secure our border, protect our daughters, and stop the Left’s sick agenda dead in its tracks. **She’s not here to make friends. She’s here to make history.** Nancy is fighting to:
* Eliminate South Carolina’s Income Tax—the highest in the Southeast
* Prosecute violent criminals and pedophiles with zero leniency
* Crack down on sanctuary sheriffs who let illegal aliens roam free
* Defund radical public universities that indoctrinate, not educate
* Reform education so students learn how to think—not what to think
* Work hand-in-hand with President Trump to put South Carolina FIRST

Through every hardship, Nancy never gave up. And now she’s ready to do what she’s always done: roll up her sleeves, take the heat, and lead from the front. **South Carolina is worth fighting for. And Nancy Mace is just getting started.**
---
| |

name: Roy Cooper
subject: From my family to yours, Merry Christmas and Happy New Year!
body:
A season filled with peace, joy, and endless hope.
|
# [](https://act.roycooper.com/go/2365?source=&t=1&akid=217%2E124072%2ElzelCF)
Dear Peter, Today, as we celebrate the holidays, I want to send my best wishes to you and your loved ones. **During this season, my hope for the future remains unbounded because I see every day that so many people are willing to do so much in service to others.** From the teachers who inspire our next generation to the first responders who answer every call to the members of our military who keep this nation safe, there are so many people around us who devote themselves to giving back. I’m grateful for them during this holiday season and throughout the year. Merry Christmas and Happy New Year, Roy Cooper [](https://act.roycooper.com/go/3247?t=7&akid=217%2E124072%2ElzelCF)
* * *
Democrat Roy Cooper is running for U.S. Senate to help take back the Majority for Democrats. North Carolina is Democrats’ best chance to flip a seat from red to blue — the path to stopping Republicans in Washington starts here.

National Republicans will target North Carolina as a key battleground and invest heavily in defeating us.

Cooper is a former state legislator, Attorney General, and most recently Governor of North Carolina. North Carolina will be the most competitive Senate election of 2026, and Cooper enters the race as the most formidable candidate of either party.

Cooper’s signature achievements include helping to expand Medicaid and helping make NC the top state for business by creating thousands of jobs and growing the clean energy economy.

**[Donate today to help win this seat and flip the Senate blue.](https://act.roycooper.com/go/1914?source=&t=2&akid=217%2E124072%2ElzelCF)** | [DONATE](https://act.roycooper.com/go/1914?source=&t=3&akid=217%2E124072%2ElzelCF)
---

[SHOP OUR MERCH STORE](https://act.roycooper.com/go/2133?t=5&akid=217%2E124072%2ElzelCF)
[info@roycooper.com](mailto:info@roycooper.com) | [Privacy Policy](https://act.roycooper.com/go/825?t=6&akid=217%2E124072%2ElzelCF)
Cooper Victory Fund
PO Box 1190
Raleigh, NC 27602
Paid for by Cooper Victory Fund, a joint fundraising committee authorized by Cooper for North Carolina, DSCC, and North Carolina Democratic Party — Federal.
---
Contributions or gifts to Cooper Victory Fund are not tax deductible.
The first $7,000 of each contribution from a person and the first $10,000 from a multicandidate committee (“PAC”) will be allocated to Cooper for North Carolina. The first $3,500 of a person’s contribution and the first $5,000 of a PAC’s contribution will be designated for the primary election; the next $3,500/$5,000 will be designated for the general election. The next $10,000 from a person and $5,000 from a PAC will be allocated to North Carolina Democratic Party – Federal. The next $44,300 of a contribution from a person and the next $15,000 from a PAC will be allocated to the DSCC General Fund. The next $132,900 from a person and the next $45,000 from a PAC will be allocated to the DSCC Headquarters Fund. The next $132,900 from a person and the next $45,000 from a PAC will be allocated to the DSCC Legal Fund. A contributor may designate his or her contribution for a particular participant. The allocation formula above may change if following it would result in an excessive contribution.
Any donor history information in this email reflects what we have on file for this specific email address. If you have donated with a different email address, with a check, or with a spouse — thank you so much! We have that on file and cannot thank you enough for supporting this campaign.
By clicking the links above, you certify that you are responsible for paying all charges incurred in using the debit or credit card to be charged, and that your personal funds will be the true source of the contribution.

[You can unsubscribe from this mailing list at any time.](http://act.roycooper.com/cms/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?akid=217%2E124072%2ElzelCF)
If you wish to receive fewer messages from us, [submit this form to let us know.](https://act.roycooper.com/signup/i-want-less-emails-cooper-north-carolina/?t=8&akid=217%2E124072%2ElzelCF)

name: Greg Landsman
subject: Merry Christmas
body:
| | |
---
| | , Christmas is here. As a dad of two teenagers, I know this day means something different to every family - whether it's opening presents, going to church, serving others, or just being together.Whatever Christmas means to you, I hope you get to spend it with the people you love. This year has been tough for a lot of families. Groceries cost too much. Heating bills are too high. Parents are working harder than ever and still struggling to get ahead.That's why I come to work every day in Congress - to fight for hardworking families who deserve an economy that works for them, not just the super wealthy and big corporations. But today isn't about politics or policy. Today is about gratitude, love, and community.To everyone celebrating: Merry Christmas. To everyone working today - nurses, police officers, firefighters, and everyone else keeping our community safe and running - thank you.Cincinnati is special because of people like you who show up, care about each other, and never give up on making things better.Appreciate you,Greg
---
| | Landsman for Congress
PO Box 413
Cincinnati, OH 45201
United States
---

And to serve others, particularly the vulnerable among us. **I consider myself lucky to have met so many Coloradans who have committed their lives to service in some way.** Whether you teach future generations, work as a first responder, serve in our military, or take time for civic and community leadership, I’m inspired by your commitment. And I’m grateful for your efforts. If you celebrate today, my family wishes yours a wonderful Christmas holiday. With gratitude, Phil

| [](https://substack.com/redirect/940c4e1f-6988-4e80-b636-2832646392fc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* * *
_**by Cliff Schecter**_
Trump Derangement Syndrome (TDS), that oh-so catchy phrase tossed around by those living in the everyday MAGA Reality Show, "4 Minutes Without Oxygen And Counting," _is real_. Real, I tell you!
Look, if TDS--which Trump, in all his sanity, maturity, and empathy, [tweeted was the reason Rob Reiner was killed (it was not) right after Rob Reiner was killed](https://substack.com/redirect/2aa3d8b4-fc04-4697-be6f-79114905cc5c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)--means you're consumed with hatred for a serial conman, who spent more time with Jeffrey Epstein than Ghislaine Maxwell, oh, I got it.
Hell, 64% of the country seems to be taking this road away from perdition with me. The symptoms of TDS include loathing Trump because he deported your sister, told your wife at her government job on a Friday at 4:45 pm not to bother coming in next week--or ever again--has more spasms on tariffs than during a Shark Week he spent with Stormy, and has bankrupted more small businesses than heart disease.
Other symptoms: despising Trump for being a cerebrally perforated ass-monger who screams at female reporters for asking basic questions, like he's a coyote with Lyssaviruses. Knowing he fills his diaper faster than he ever did, The Trump Taj Mahal, calling American soldiers killed in war "suckers and losers," and thinking Stealth Fighters were invisible, like Wonder Woman's fucking plane.
Yes, the numb-chud actually believed that.
* * *
[Save 40% & help us build for 2026!](https://substack.com/redirect/940c4e1f-6988-4e80-b636-2832646392fc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* * *
You may also fall into TDS if you have a strong dislike for adjudicated rapists, [Jan 6th insurrectionists, Putin-suckling seditionists](https://substack.com/redirect/1e78f3c2-c70a-4a70-8a3c-3562be69254c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), and golden-showering onanists. TDS can also become inflamed by hearing un-sweater'd, White Bill Cosby brag about sleeping with his "friend's wives," being a convicted felon, and making the Oval Office look like it was decorated by Pizarro.
You could also catch TDS just by thinking about the fact he told us to inject bleach, stared directly into an eclipse, ran a scam university and charity, and after like 112 years of practice still ties his tie so it hangs below his d*k. It's easy to come down with TDS if you think about the fact that [Sleepy McPutinLick](https://substack.com/redirect/1e78f3c2-c70a-4a70-8a3c-3562be69254c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) allowed an additional [400,000 Americans to die from COVID](https://substack.com/redirect/5ffeefd2-3b63-4014-8d86-0826962bfc13?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) due to lies about masks, vaccines, and general malaise.
But at least he picked for HHS Secretary a roid-raged, former 16-year heroin addict who thinks it's vaccines are perilous products to inject. Those GD things are risky, dangerous, unsafe to RFK Jr! Like being faithful to a spouse.
Maybe Jr. could've gotten on curing TDS things, but he was too busy road-tripping with a brisk wind breezing through his locks, a worm hanging out his eardrum, and a trusty whale head sturdily attached to his jeep. All while writing dry-heave-inducing-love poetry to a former "Look at me, look at me!" _New York Magazine_ "reporter" is more drunk on her mirror-image than he is.
Look, let's get straight to the point here. **Yes, it must totes be TDS to absolutely despise the literal worst "human" shuffling his cankles growing out of his cankles around America.** A vile, venal, fragile, flabby, narcissistic, lazy-ass, selfish, sociopathic, soul-holed, gauche, gaudy, perpetually dumbf*ked man, woman, person, camera old coot who looks like someone piled a burnt-ochre-colored watermelon on top of a jello gelatin factory spill.
An international laughingstock, a domestic terrorist-inspiring cultist, and someone who spits out tweets from his golden crapper at 3 am in 3rd grade English. How could anyone dislike that?? It must be a disease.
Well, I proudly cop to it. As I do PPDS, or Pol Pot Derangement Syndrome. And SOSDS, of Son of Sam Derangement Syndrome, who made 1979 a pretty shitty year in New York.
* * *
Does the Trumpconomy have you down? Subscribe for free!
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuYmx1ZWFtcC5jby9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.01ETFkCL5XdSoexvVXqjaPm12hEu53C4r8wLge7xahA?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=182367522)
* * *
Others who it must be derangement I hate, not normal human impulses, include--and this list is by no means complete: Mobuto Sese Seko, Stephen Miller, Viktor Orban, anchovies, David Koresh, the NRA, Suharto, the clown from _It,_ J.D. Vance, Jack Palance in pretty much any Western, Byron de La Beckwith--and the as-creepy actor who played him, James Woods--Sean Hannity, Bolsonaro, Osama bin Laden, Vigo The Carpathian, Hitler, Stalin, Mussolini, Cricket-shooting Kristi Noem and kale.
Hate 'em all. My apologies to the kale lovers out there. My non-apologies to the J.D. Vance lover out there. And look, I could go on. But I think you get the basic point. Trump Derangement Syndrome is another in a long list of stupid right-wing talking points, as if it takes some kind of affliction to despise convicted criminals, killers, and other all-around awful homosapiens.
You Christians, you got this really cool guy, Jesus. We're celebrating his Birthday (I mean, it wasn't really his birthday if you look into history and all that, but who really cares, he was definitely a cool dude).
Now I'm not a religious adherent, but Jesus said some pretty cool stuff, beyond radical for his time. For example, The Parable of the Stranger. We might want to staple that to Tom Homan's head (if he's being honest with himself, it can only make him better looking. And if we're being honest, he's the one member of ICE we kinda wish would wear a mask.)
But when it comes to the whole turning the other cheek thing, I've tried. I really have. And sorry, I can't.
I hate Trump, the way I hate Hitler, the way I hate Pete Hegseth.
And this doesn't make me _deranged_.
It makes me human.
* * *

| | Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw
---
Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18]

clean_body: Most retirees miss out on bigger Social Security checks—leaving billions unclaimed. | | | Don't want to receive our publications? | | | Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18] | | | | | | Most retirees miss out on bigger Social Security checks—leaving billions unclaimed. name: State Representative Ryan Chamberlin
subject: Merry Christmas from Rep. Ryan Chamberlin
body:
Merry Christmas from Rep. Ryan Chamberlin | | | [View this email in your browser](https://us21.campaign-archive.com/?e=d1c1b9864f&u=ec15c6093052c02efbd22b6cd&id=9612100404)
---
| |
---
| | **MERRY CHRISTMAS FROM REP. RYAN CHAMBERLIN**
---
|
---
|

> > Friends and Neighbors,
>
> Wishing you a Merry Christmas and a Happy New Year. As we celebrate this special season, I hope you are able to enjoy safe travels, meaningful time with loved ones, and moments of rest and reflection.
>
> This season also invites us to give thanks for the freedoms we enjoy and to remember those who protect them. I ask you to join me in praying for our veterans, active military members, law enforcement officers, first responders, and their families, who sacrifice their own comfort so that we may live safely and worship freely.
>
> This past year has been an important one for our state. I am honored to serve you in the Florida House and remain committed to keeping Florida strong, free, and prosperous. I will continue working on your behalf to protect our shared values and strengthen our communities.
>
> Thank you for the trust you place in me. Serving you is a privilege, and I am grateful for your continued support. May God bless you and your family as we celebrate the birth of Christ and look ahead to a hopeful new year.
>
>
> Ryan Chamberlin
> State Representative
> District 24
---
| |
---

name: Tim Burchett
subject: A Tender Tennessee Christmas
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Burchett for Congress**
body:
🧡🎄✝️

| | | |
---
| | Dear Friend,
---
---
As my friend Gary Chapman sings in his iconic song, “A tender Tennessee Christmas is the only Christmas for me.”

That line always hits home and brings a tear to my eye.

Christmas isn’t about fancy decorations or big plans—it’s about celebrating the birth of our Savior with the ones you love, wherever you call home.

I hope your day is filled with warmth, love, and laughter.

I hope your new year is blessed.

And I hope you know how thankful I am for folks like you who love this country and trust me to fight for our values in Washington. >From the Burchett family to yours, Merry Christmas!
Sincerely,
---
Tim Burchett
---
| | |
---

clean_body: 🧡🎄✝️ | | | | | | Dear Friend, As my friend Gary Chapman sings in his iconic song, “A tender Tennessee Christmas is the only Christmas for me.” That line always hits home and brings a tear to my eye. Christmas isn’t about fancy decorations or big plans—it’s about celebrating the birth of our Savior with the ones you love, wherever you call home. I hope your day is filled with warmth, love, and laughter. I hope your new year is blessed. And I hope you know how thankful I am for folks like you who love this country and trust me to fight for our values in Washington. >From the Burchett family to yours, Merry Christmas! Sincerely, Tim Burchett | | | Stay in Touch 👇 Paid for by Burchett for Congress | | | ---|---|---

name: Michael Baumgartner
subject: Merry Christmas & Happy New Year from the Baumgartners!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Vote Baumgartner for Congress | www
body:
Grateful for a wonderful 2025.

| | | | | |
---
| |

What a year it’s been!
I want to write you before 2025 comes to an end to thank you for the incredible support you have given to me. It’s the honor of a lifetime to serve in Congress.
Anyone with a sense of history can’t help but be awed by this cathedral of democracy, working in the shadows of Sam Rayburn, Newt Gingrich, and Tom Foley ... to visit the Oval Office and attend President Trump’s address to a joint session of Congress ... to go on a run in the morning, I traverse from the Washington Monument to the Lincoln Memorial.
But the grandeur of this place must take a back seat to the real work... with Chairman Jim Jordan on the Judiciary Committee, pushing against “sanctuary state” laws; on the Higher Education Committee holding academic administrators accountable for woke ideology on their campuses; and on the Foreign Affairs Committee working for bipartisan solutions that put America First and protect us from the ongoing threat of radical Islamic terrorism.
With the smallest majority in almost a century, Republicans have managed to stop the largest tax increase in history, opened up vast tracks to increased US energy production, and given businesses the tools they need to grow.
No doubt you’ve heard of our 2025 Congressional successes like reversing the electric car mandate, new measures to reduce taxes on tips, on overtime and on Senior Citizens receiving social security. But, did you know that we also created a new $1,700 education tax credit that can be used by parents for any school - private or public - that they choose and that we provided enough funding to finally finish the border wall and begin implementation of the new “Golden Dome” missile defense system to protect America here at home?
It is an honor to be part of the most consequential changes in the federal government since WWII. There have been missteps along the way, but the new direction is clear: America is going to be strong, not shy about our interests overseas, encouraging robust free enterprise here at home, while shrinking the size and scope of the federal bureaucracy. In this, I feel we follow in the footsteps of Presidents Eisenhower and Reagan but we can go further and faster.
Over the last year it has been wonderful to see so many of you at our campaign events - Baumtoberfest, The Garden Party, Paint it Red, our Cherry Blossom event in DC and our recent Christmas Party.
There will be more to come in 2026 and I particularly hope that many of you get a chance to visit me in Washington, D.C.
You know that President Trump is pulling out all the stops to celebrate the 250th anniversary of the Declaration of Independence. There will be a parade, a “state fair” with exhibits from all 50 states, sporting events, and what promises to be the largest fireworks display in the world on our national mall.
It may be another 250 years before you will have an opportunity like this, so mark your calendar for a Baumgartner Campaign event in Washington DC on July 4, 2026 if you can make it out!
Members of Congress are tasked with keeping the ideals of democracy alive through each generation, a government of the people, by the people, for the people. Your support makes all the difference. Thank you.
Being a member of Congress is not always the easiest job in the world and all the flights, the time away from home and the public aspects of partisanship the family sometimes has to experience can be a test, but Eleanor and I want you to know how grateful we are for this opportunity and the role you play in allowing us to have it.
We get to live in the greatest, freest, most wonderful country in the history of the world, and we are bound and determined that we are going to keep it that way for our five kids and your children as well.
2025 has been a great start, and the best is yet to come.
From our family to yours, may you have a very Blessed & Merry Christmas, filled with kindness and love, and a New Year that is prosperous and serene.
Michael & Eleanor
---
| | | | |
---
| | Paid for by Vote Baumgartner for Congress | www.votebaumgartner.com

Our mailing address is: Vote Baumgartner for Congress, PO Box 8508, Spokane, WA 99203
---

---

name: Jonathan Nez
subject: Merry Christmas and Happy Holidays, Peter!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jonathan Nez for Congress
body:
If you’d like to unsubscribe, [click here.](https://secure.ngpvan.com/p/ujh3rCyCjUaeL1qnnX77UA2?unsubscribedata=p%2BZ9UTRWC7hb%2FQ7BYJNfD3R9M0EH6YHu5WUjNe3nnlMQcQpp%2BJ8bVTWdEqip6DszZ7XmPm%2FUwQiHae32uhzhGR7aYarBEdQ6fNV%2FOQ%2FX9vYXmJMmOqxHLXWBM7R19e7%2BgYSy3SfqvA3Cf8bASU2oAWiFscaCIzZJkYhyrpli8%2BLA3%2FIFFoxYqZaMq9uWPY9SKzWVmg49BN5jPnKYFblgDGZtIKZvhh%2BKuu%2FAH15smBENoMEDOY8NenIIO7bw%2BCcuWSxiSeGbj3eAMlsfYbt1OVzqf6JrKaEgpPzvGoVijRA0rP6MgH1DGcAOd4bVjDf2Qzpj00ho%2BVGulUj20kHtClzmfuKu495C3kO%2FgXaMmlXwNQ7jzb4sEXHyOJhi5p3klgGUBABbLYNJ%2B88ZuQ6yngbbk97g7lcjW0AXEn7YwojUM8CjbUQUWkYqfnt9HAfF)
---
| |
---
Hello Peter, and Yáʼátʼééh Késhmish! On behalf of Phefelia and our family I want to wish you a Merry Christmas and a blessed holiday season. No matter how you celebrate, I hope you’ll have time to rest and enjoy the company of relatives and friends. It’s a wonderful opportunity to reconnect with our elders and those who live far away, and I look forward every year to seeing those who travel across the Navajo Nation to be present for the holidays. To our warriors who are serving overseas, we wish you peace and strength as you keep our nation safe.

As a man of faith, today is an especially important holiday for my family — and this season gives us a time to reflect on how we can be better stewards of our neighbors and communities. Here in rural Arizona, we pride ourselves on being independent and self-sufficient, but we also look out for our neighbors and community members who need a hand up.

These are challenging times for many folks, and it’s important that we always remember those who are struggling in the face of hunger, addiction, or any other adversity this time of year. We pray for these souls, and remember the example of empathy and generosity set by the man whose birthday we celebrate today.

May you have a peaceful and joyous holiday season. I feel so blessed to have you by my side as we build this movement for a brighter future for rural Arizona.

Ahéhee' and thank you,

Jonathan Nez

name: Support for Our Troops
subject: Wish our heroes a Merry Christmas
body:
Last chance to sign our card and show your support.
[](https://www.uso.org)
[SIGN THE CARD](https://links.e.uso.)
---
[](https://links.e.uso.)
---

[**Will you sign the card for our service members
spending Christmas far from their loved ones?**](https://links.e.uso.)
[](https://links.e.uso.)
**Merry Christmas.**
We hope you are celebrating surrounded by the warmth of family and loved ones.
We also hope you'll take a minute to think of the thousands of service members deployed right now far away from their own families. Today, like every day, is a day they are putting country before self -- willing to sacrifice everything for our freedom. For _us_.
**Just one small step on this special day can make all the difference. You can make sure they know they 're not alone -- that patriots like you are wishing them a Merry Christmas. **
"We really just are looking for something to take our minds off the stressful situations that we are encountering here," one service member said.
**[That's why we set a goal to reach 100,000 signatures on this Christmas card for service members -- but we're falling short, and your name is on the list of those who still haven't signed.](https://links.e.uso.org/els/v2/Dd9Kh-2683t4/Ty9QWGF3eFIvTkpzQklSRTRSTEZGa1dHWkFSMnVwb3pJVEVoS0p1bXlFNHdDSUlsVnl6SWE2eGhHZDQxbUFXSlBJMkRnbkxGWTZ1QVB5TmR2T3NrSk9EaHJvM1lxbEN0d1pYTU5Cb1RsZ2c9S0/TXpYVDdLQzdsbnRjMUtURVVjZFZIeGxTQmI0WTVzNkE4blVpTENzRktnaEZSRG5RazBPYWJSTHZMWjRsZFFoRlhtdWJ2SFVTbWg1aEZxaWY1N0RqQTBEMVMyd1Y5a2Y5S0)**
[ADD YOUR NAME](https://links.e.uso.)
---
**Thank you for taking the time out of your own Christmas celebrations to make sure our troops feel valued and supported this holiday season and beyond.**
**[Your note this Christmas will mean so much to the troops serving our country right now.](https://links.e.uso.org/els/v2/Dd9Kh-2683t4/Ty9QWGF3eFIvTkpzQklSRTRSTEZGa1dHWkFSMnVwb3pJVEVoS0p1bXlFNHdDSUlsVnl6SWE2eGhHZDQxbUFXSlBJMkRnbkxGWTZ1QVB5TmR2T3NrSk9EaHJvM1lxbEN0d1pYTU5Cb1RsZ2c9S0/TXpYVDdLQzdsbnRjMUtURVVjZFZIeGxTQmI0WTVzNkE4blVpTENzRktnaEZSRG5RazBPYWJSTHZMWjRsZFFoRlhtdWJ2SFVTbWg1aEZxaWY1N0RqQTBEMVMyd1Y5a2Y5S0)**
[SIGN THE CARD NOW](https://links.e.uso.)
---
It will be shown in USO Centers across the world to reach our troops during this time. Thank you for spreading Christmas cheer!
Merry Christmas,
The USO

clean_body: Last chance to sign our card and show your support. Merry Christmas. We hope you are celebrating surrounded by the warmth of family and loved ones. We also hope you'll take a minute to think of the thousands of service members deployed right now far away from their own families. Today, like every day, is a day they are putting country before self -- willing to sacrifice everything for our freedom. For us. Just one small step on this special day can make all the difference. You can make sure they know they 're not alone -- that patriots like you are wishing them a Merry Christmas. "We really just are looking for something to take our minds off the stressful situations that we are encountering here," one service member said. Thank you for taking the time out of your own Christmas celebrations to make sure our troops feel valued and supported this holiday season and beyond. It will be shown in USO Centers across the world to reach our troops during this time. Thank you for spreading Christmas cheer! Merry Christmas, The USO United Service Organizations, Inc. | | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.
---

Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671.

Atlantic Ave., Suite 200 Delray Beach, FL 33444 Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671. | | You are receiving this newsletter from Your Capital Minds (YCM), an esteemed member of the Stark Media LLC family. name: Ami Bera
subject: Wishing you a happy holiday season
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Bera for Congress
body:
| Peter —

As we come to the close of another year, I want to wish you and your loved ones a very happy holiday season and a healthy, hopeful New Year.

The holidays are a time to reflect on what truly matters — family, faith, service, and community. It’s also a reminder that even in challenging times, we are stronger when we come together with kindness and a shared commitment to moving forward.

I am deeply grateful for the trust you place in me and for the many ways you contribute to our community. Your engagement, ideas, and support make it possible to continue working toward a future where the American Dream is in reach for everyone.

As we look ahead to the new year, I remain committed to serving with integrity and focusing on the issues that matter most to hardworking families in the Sacramento region and across our nation.

However you celebrate, I hope this season brings you peace, joy, and time with those you cherish most.

Be well,

Ami Bera

P.S. This holiday season, I’m thinking of the folks who might not have a hot meal waiting for them. As inflation and costs continue to rise, it’s more important than ever that we step up to support our neighbors in need, [**and I hope you’ll join me in making a contribution to the Sacramento Food Bank to help feed hungry families.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121972664/591170147/1537790662?source=EM_NBI_AB_20251225_EN_HolidayMessage_0_12MO_AmiBera_NA_NA_NA&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS85MDU3OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4OWJjZjFjOC1kMWUxLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=htFqC-d9KRE_hcpEoydmNl0iHxgynD2GXr5xyeQSlyo=&emci=d4d9a11d-45e0-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=89bcf1c8-d1e1-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ceid=32394541#!/donation/checkout)

name: From Trump
subject: I’m in your chimney!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Trump National Committee JFC, Inc
body:
Merry Christmas - I sent you a special Christmas letter!
| | | | FROM TRUMP:
**I SENT YOU A SPECIAL CHRISTMAS LETTER **
---
[](https://link.win.donaldjtrump.)
But, my team says that it went to the wrong address...

So, I guess I’ll stick to emails from here on out.

[HERE’S WHAT I SAID:](https://link.win.donaldjtrump.)
Dear Friend,
Today, I want to wish you and your family a very Merry Christmas.
**Your support has meant more to me than I can say. Because of patriots like you, we are still standing strong.**
As a small token of my appreciation, I have a special Christmas surprise gift I would like to send directly to my supporters. **_[Please, confirm your address here:>](https://link.win.donaldjtrump.)_**
Please consider this my way of saying thank you, Merry Christmas, and God bless you for standing with me when our country needs it the most.
[CONFIRM ADDRESS](https://link.win.donaldjtrump.)
**_[I would deeply appreciate it if you chipped in any amount: it can be SMALL or very GENEROUS before my end-of-year fundraising deadline.>](https://link.win.donaldjtrump.)_**
With YOU & the full support of the MAGA Movement, we will continue the fight to protect the America we cherish.
**Merry Christmas to you and your family,**
**And may God bless the United States of America.**
Sincerely,
Donald J. Trump

[](https://link.win.donaldjtrump.)

subject: Merry Christmas from our family to yours!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Steve Daines for Montana
body:
Wishing you a Christmas and New Year brimming with peace, laughter, and precious time spent with the ones you hold dear. |
---

Fellow American - From the Daines family to yours, we want to wish you a Merry Christmas!

As the holiday season wraps our homes in warmth and joy, we would like to pause and sincerely thank you for your steadfast support and commitment.

Wishing you a Christmas and New Year brimming with peace, laughter, and precious time spent with the ones you hold dear.

Merry Christmas,

Cindy and Steve Daines

**BREAKING via _NBC_ :** "Trump just realigned the entire political map. Democrats have 'no easy path' to fix it."
[**Elizabeth Warren is calling for an END to the Electoral College now. Without 125,OOO signatures, ****we won 't be able to show Democrats in Congress there's a critical mass. Please sign the petition now:**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/71060?t=7&akid=23742%2E4449941%2E0xYbHD)
[SIGN NOW >>](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/71060?t=8&akid=23742%2E4449941%2E0xYbHD)
---
This petition is registered to you only.

Donald Trump only became President in 2016 because of the outdated Electoral College.

_Hillary Clinton won the popular vote by nearly 2.9 million. But the Electoral College overruled her popular vote victory._

**Now, Sen. Elizabeth Warren is urgently pleading with Democrats:**
We couldn't agree more. But now that Trump is in office, he and his Republican cronies will do everything in their power to uphold the Electoral College.

**So we need to act now -- because we must prevent unpopular, unqualified candidates from EVER becoming President again.**

[**_If every Democrat reading this signs our petition, we will reach our 125,OOO signature goal within minutes and gain traction for this urgent legislation in Congress. Please add your name immediately:_**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/71060?t=9&akid=23742%2E4449941%2E0xYbHD)
[SIGN NOW >>](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/71060?t=10&akid=23742%2E4449941%2E0xYbHD)
---
Thank you,

_Fight for Reform_

| | Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw
---
Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18]
